            "+# V11.0 \"HPC-SDG\" Build & Deployment Certification Checklist\n",
            "+\n",
            "+## Introduction: Mission and Mandate\n",
            "+This document serves as the definitive certification checklist for the V11.0 \"HPC-SDG\" suite. The V11.0 release represents a critical strategic pivot, engineered to resolve the catastrophic pipeline deadlocks, scientific paradoxes, and performance blockers that defined the V10.x campaign. This checklist codifies the non-negotiable architectural mandates, physics implementations, and operational standards required to stabilize the platform. Successful validation against every criterion herein signifies that the core architecture is sound, the physics engine is mathematically sovereign, and the integrated system is mission-ready to commence the autonomous \"Parametric Search for Critical Resonance.\"\n",
            "+\n",
            "+---\n",
            "+\n",
            "+## 1.0 Foundational Architectural Integrity\n",
            "+This section addresses the root causes of the V10.x pipeline failures, including orchestration deadlocks and Just-in-Time (JIT) compilation thrashing. The criteria outlined below are designed to establish a resilient, high-performance computational foundation capable of supporting all subsequent scientific objectives. Adherence to these architectural mandates is non-negotiable.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 1.1 Unified Hashing Mandate\n",
            "+**Criterion:** System implements the Unified Hashing Mandate to resolve the \"Orchestrator-Hunter Desynchronization\" deadlock that plagued V10.x.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+1. **Deterministic Generation:** The orchestrator's hashing function is modified to remove all non-deterministic elements (e.g., `time.time()`). It must generate a deterministic hash, which serves as the run's Universally Unique Identifier (UUID), derived solely from the input parameter set.\n",
            "+2. **Central Authority Passing:** The orchestrator explicitly passes this single, calculated UUID as a command-line argument (`--job_uuid`) to both the Worker (`worker_sncgl_sdg.py`) and Validator (`validation_pipeline_v11.py`) subprocesses. It is confirmed that these downstream components are mandated to receive and use this centrally-generated identifier for all artifact input/output (I/O) operations.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 1.2 Decoupled Two-Layer Execution\n",
            "+**Criterion:** Architecture strictly enforces the mandated two-layer design, preventing I/O-bound analysis from stalling the primary research campaign.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+- **Layer 1 (JAX-Optimized HPC Core):** This layer is reserved exclusively for the high-throughput, JAX-native physics loop. It consists solely of the Hunter AI (`aste_hunter`), the S-NCGL Worker, and the SDG Geometric Solver.\n",
            "+- **Layer 2 (Decoupled Secondary Analysis Suite):** All high-overhead, non-JAX, and I/O-bound analysis tasks are formally demoted to asynchronous, post-processing components. This includes BSSN constraint checking, Topological Data Analysis (TDA), and visualization generation. These components operate only after the main simulation artifact has been successfully written to disk.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 1.3 JAX HPC Compliance\n",
            "+**Criterion:** Implementation of the three existential JAX High-Performance Computing (HPC) mandates required to make the co-evolutionary system computationally tractable is confirmed.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+1. **Control Flow Refactor:** Python `for` loops within the core time-stepping logic have been replaced with `jax.lax.scan`. This allows JAX to compile the entire simulation loop into a single, highly optimized XLA computational graph, eliminating catastrophic \"compilation thrashing.\"\n",
            "+2. **State Management:** Simulation state and parameters are managed using JAX-compliant pytrees, specifically `NamedTuple` data structures. This resolves critical JIT compilation failures related to non-hashable static arguments and ensures auditable parameter provenance.\n",
            "+3. **Vectorized Operations:** `jax.vmap` is used for spatial tensor operations, such as inverting the metric field (`g_{\\mu\\nu}`). This vectorizes the function to run in parallel across all spatial points, resolving `TypeError` issues and unlocking massive performance gains.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+With the system's architectural stability confirmed, the focus now shifts to certifying the correctness of the physics engine itself.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+---\n",
            "+\n",
            "+## 2.0 Physics Core Implementation & Unification\n",
            "+The V11.0 release constitutes a complete \"engine swap,\" a strategic pivot from the falsified BSSN solver to the axiomatically correct SDG solver. This section verifies that this transition has been successfully implemented, ensuring that the simulation is, for the first time, solving the mathematically sovereign physics of the IRER framework. Successful validation here confirms the project has achieved \"Foundational Closure.\"\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 2.1 S-NCGL Axiomatic Derivation\n",
            "+**Criterion:** The Sourced, Non-Local Complex Ginzburg-Landau (S-NCGL) master equation is implemented as a direct axiomatic derivation, no longer as a \"borrowed analogue.\"\n",
            "+\n",
            "+- Verification confirms that the implementation in `worker_sncgl_sdg.py` is directly derived from the canonical Lagrangian density, $\\mathcal{L}_{\\text{FMIA}}$. This action resolves both the \"Formalism Gap\" and the \"Parameter Provenance Gap,\" establishing a direct mathematical line from the theory's first principles to the simulation's executable code.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 2.2 SDG Solver Integration\n",
            "+**Criterion:** The legacy Baumgarte-Shapiro-Shibata-Nakamura (BSSN) solver is fully replaced by the JAX-native Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) solver.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+- Verification confirms the BSSN check (`validation_pipeline_bssn.py`) has been formally demoted to a \"Classical GR Benchmark.\" Its execution has been moved to the asynchronous Layer 2 analysis suite, ensuring it no longer gates the primary simulation loop or flags high-fidelity S-NCGL solutions as \"physically impossible.\"\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 2.3 End-to-End Differentiable Simulation\n",
            "+**Criterion:** Integration of the JAX-native S-NCGL and SDG solvers creates an end-to-end differentiable simulation environment.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+- This is confirmed as a critical capability upgrade. It enables the `aste_hunter` AI to utilize `jax.grad` to receive gradients directly from the computed emergent spacetime geometry ($g_{\\mu\\nu}$). This allows the Hunter to actively steer the parameter search toward geometrically stable solutions, effectively using the emergent geometry itself as a high-resolution fitness function and steering away from the \"numerical stiffness\" regions that plagued the V10.x campaign.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+A correct physics engine must be paired with a rigorous and streamlined validation process to be effective.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+---\n",
            "+\n",
            "+## 3.0 Streamlined Validation & Governance\n",
            "+The V11.0 validation pipeline is engineered as a streamlined, high-speed component of the HPC core. Its sole mandate is to calculate and report only the essential metrics required for the Hunter AI's fitness evaluation. This stands in stark contrast to the high-overhead, multi-modal scientific analysis that has been correctly relegated to the decoupled Layer 2 suite.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 3.1 Core Scientific & Physical Metrics\n",
            "+**Criterion:** `validation_pipeline_v11.py` calculates and reports the two core metrics used for fitness evaluation.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+- **Scientific Fidelity:** The Log-Prime Sum of Squared Errors (SSE), which quantitatively measures the simulation's alignment with the framework's core falsifiable prediction.\n",
            "+- **Physical Order:** The Phase Coherence Score (PCS), which measures the degree of ordered structure formation and acts as a proxy for a fundamental conserved charge.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 3.2 Axiomatic Integrity Check\n",
            "+**Criterion:** Validation pipeline performs the \"Noetherian Integrity Check.\"\n",
            "+\n",
            "+- This test verifies that the numerical evolution scheme respects the fundamental symmetries of the axiomatically derived physics. The foundational Lagrangian $\\mathcal{L}_{\\text{FMIA}}$ is invariant under global gauge rotation, which, by Noether’s Theorem, guarantees a conserved quantity representing total \"Informational Coherence\" ($Q_{\\text{coherence}}$). The pipeline monitors the conservation of this quantity (using the PCS score as a proxy), providing a powerful internal validation of the physics engine's integrity.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 3.3 Data Contract Adherence\n",
            "+**Criterion:** The end-to-end data contract between all Layer 1 components is intact and immutable.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+- Verification confirms that `validation_pipeline_v11.py` correctly receives the `--job_uuid` command-line argument.\n",
            "+- It is confirmed that the validator uses this UUID to deterministically locate and load the `simulation_data_{job_uuid}.h5` artifact.\n",
            "+- It is confirmed that the validator successfully writes its findings to a correctly named `provenance_{job_uuid}.json` file for consumption by the Hunter AI and the monitoring system.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+A fully validated build must be deployable within a functional and responsive orchestration environment.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+---\n",

            "+- Verification confirms that the `/api/start-hunt` endpoint launches the core engine (`core_engine.execute_hunt()`) in a background thread.\n",
            "+- This architecture correctly ensures the API returns an immediate HTTP 202 (Accepted) response, freeing the UI from the computational workload.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+### 4.2 Asynchronous Monitoring via Filesystem Watcher\n",
            "+**Criterion:** The asynchronous monitoring mechanism is functional.\n",
            "+\n",
            "+- Verification confirms that a `ProvenanceWatcher` class, implemented using the `watchdog` library, runs in its own dedicated background thread.\n",
            "+- This watcher correctly monitors the filesystem for newly created `provenance_*.json` files. Upon detection, it safely reads the artifact, extracts key metrics, and updates a central `hub_status.json` file for polling by the UI.\n",
            "+\n",

            "     # Penalize flat or zero-density results\n",
            "     if variance < 1e-5: return 997.0\n",
            " \n",
            "     # Mock SSE based on variance as a proxy for structure\n",
            "     mock_sse = 1.0 / (1.0 + 100 * variance)\n",
            "     return float(mock_sse)\n",
            " \n",
            " def calculate_pcs(rho_final_state: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
            "     \"\"\"Calculates the Phase Coherence Score (PCS).\"\"\"\n",
            "     if scipy_coherence is None: return 0.0\n",
            "     try:\n",
            "         if rho_final_state.ndim < 2 or rho_final_state.shape[0] < 4: return 0.0\n",
            "         # Extract two distinct parallel rays\n",
            "         ray_1 = rho_final_state[rho_final_state.shape[0] // 4, :]\n",
            "         ray_2 = rho_final_state[3 * rho_final_state.shape[0] // 4, :]\n",
            "         \n",
            "         _, Cxy = scipy_coherence(ray_1, ray_2)\n",
            "         pcs_score = np.mean(Cxy)\n",
            "         return float(pcs_score) if not np.isnan(pcs_score) else 0.0\n",
            "     except Exception:\n",
            "         return 0.0\n",
            " \n",

            " def calculate_bssn_h_norm(rho_state: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
            "     \"\"\"\n",
            "     Calculates the L2 norm of the BSSN Hamiltonian constraint violation.\n",
            "     This function numerically implements the constraint check on a given rho\n",
            "     field state, returning the H-Norm L2 metric.\n",
            "     \"\"\"\n",
            "     if rho_state.ndim < 2:\n",
            "         return np.nan\n",
            "     gradients = np.gradient(rho_state)\n",
            "     laplacian = sum(np.gradient(g)[i] for i, g in enumerate(gradients))\n",
            "     curvature = rho_state + laplacian\n",
            "     h_norm = np.sqrt(np.mean(curvature**2))\n",
            "     return float(h_norm)\n",
            " \n",
            " \n",

            " \n",
            "     # Penalize flat or zero-density results\n",
            "     if variance < 1e-5: return 997.0\n",
            " \n",
            "     # Mock SSE based on variance as a proxy for structure\n",
            "     mock_sse = 1.0 / (1.0 + 100 * variance)\n",
            "     return float(mock_sse)\n",
            " \n",
            " def calculate_pcs(rho_final_state: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
            "     \"\"\"Calculates the Phase Coherence Score (PCS).\"\"\"\n",
            "     if scipy_coherence is None: return 0.0\n",
            "     try:\n",
            "         if rho_final_state.ndim < 2 or rho_final_state.shape[0] < 4: return 0.0\n",
            "         # Extract two distinct parallel rays\n",
            "         ray_1 = rho_final_state[rho_final_state.shape[0] // 4, :]\n",
            "         ray_2 = rho_final_state[3 * rho_final_state.shape[0] // 4, :]\n",
            "         \n",
            "         _, Cxy = scipy_coherence(ray_1, ray_2)\n",
            "         pcs_score = np.mean(Cxy)\n",
            "         return float(pcs_score) if not np.isnan(pcs_score) else 0.0\n",
            "     except Exception:\n",
            "         return 0.0\n",
            " \n",

            "index 3d63f83b407ede7df714528bae5e8b7dc6c28afb..1ec7f9212b35fac35f74fa884b8a582563243da0 100644\n",
            "--- a/draft 3/worker_sncgl_sdg.py        \n",
            "+++ b/draft 3/worker_sncgl_sdg.py        \n",
            "@@ -183,57 +183,57 @@ def run_simulation(params_path: str) -> tuple[float, float, float, jnp.ndarray,\n",
            "         spatial_resolution=grid_size,\n",
            "         sdg_alpha=params[\"sdg_alpha\"],\n",
            "         sdg_rho_vac=params[\"sdg_rho_vac\"],\n",
            "         dt=dt\n",
            "     )\n",
            " \n",
            "     initial_carry = (Psi_initial, rho_s_initial, g_initial)\n",
            "     (final_Psi, final_rho_s, final_g_munu), _ = jax.lax.scan(rk4_step_fn, initial_carry, None, length=steps)\n",
            "     \n",
            "     # Ensure computation is finished before stopping timer\n",
            "     final_Psi.block_until_ready()\n",
            "     duration = time.time() - start_time\n",
            "     \n",
            "     # Calculate final metrics from simulation state (now using the final state from RK4)\n",
            "     sse_metric = calculate_final_sse(final_Psi)\n",
            "     h_norm = calculate_h_norm(final_g_munu, grid_size)\n",
            "     \n",
            "     return duration, sse_metric, h_norm, final_Psi, final_rho_s, final_g_munu\n",
            " \n",
            " \n",
            " def write_results(job_uuid: str, psi_field: np.ndarray, rho_s_field: np.ndarray, g_mu_nu_field: np.ndarray, sse: float, h_norm: float):\n",
            "     \"\"\"Saves simulation output and metrics to a standardized HDF5 file.\n",
            "     Now saves final Psi, rho_s, and g_mu_nu.\n",
            "     \"\"\"\n",
            "     os.makedirs(settings.DATA_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n",
            "-    filename = os.path.join(settings.DATA_DIR, f\"rho_history_{job_uuid}.h5\")\n",
            "+    filename = os.path.join(settings.DATA_DIR, f\"simulation_data_{job_uuid}.h5\")\n",
            "     \n",
            "     with h5py.File(filename, \"w\") as f:\n",
            "         f.create_dataset(\"final_psi\", data=psi_field)\n",
            "         f.create_dataset(\"final_rho_s\", data=rho_s_field)\n",
            "         f.create_dataset(\"final_g_mu_nu\", data=g_mu_nu_field)\n",
            "         \n",
            "         # Save metrics as attributes at the root for easy access by validator\n",
            "         f.attrs[settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY] = sse\n",
            "         f.attrs[settings.STABILITY_METRIC_KEY] = h_norm\n",
            "         \n",
            "     print(f\"[Worker {job_uuid[:8]}] HDF5 artifact saved to: {filename}\")\n",
            " \n",
            " \n",
            " def main():\n",
            "     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"V11.0 S-NCGL/SDG Co-Evolution Worker (Production Final)\")\n",
            "     parser.add_argument(\"--config_path\", required=True, help=\"Path to the parameter config JSON file\")\n",
            "-    parser.add_argument(\"--config_hash\", required=True, help=\"Unique identifier for the simulation run\") # Renamed to config_hash for consistency\n",
            "+    parser.add_argument(\"--job_uuid\", required=True, help=\"Unique identifier for the simulation run derived by the orchestrator\")\n",
            "     args = parser.parse_args()\n",
            " \n",
            " \n",
            "-    print(f\"[Worker {args.config_hash[:8]}] Starting co-evolution simulation (Production Final) ...\")\n",
            "-    \n",
            "+    print(f\"[Worker {args.job_uuid[:8]}] Starting co-evolution simulation (Production Final) ...\")\n",
            "+\n",
            "     duration, sse, h_norm, final_Psi, final_rho_s, final_g_munu = run_simulation(args.config_path)\n",
            "-    \n",
            "-    print(f\"[Worker {args.config_hash[:8]}] Simulation complete in {duration:.4f}s.\")\n",
            "-    \n",
            "-    write_results(args.config_hash, np.array(final_Psi), np.array(final_rho_s), np.array(final_g_munu), sse, h_norm)\n",
            "+\n",
            "+    print(f\"[Worker {args.job_uuid[:8]}] Simulation complete in {duration:.4f}s.\")\n",
            "+\n",
            "+    write_results(args.job_uuid, np.array(final_Psi), np.array(final_rho_s), np.array(final_g_munu), sse, h_norm)\n",
            " \n",
            " \n",
            " if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
            "     main()\n",

      "source": [
        "### Summary of V11.0 HPC-SDG Certification Checklist\n",
        "\n",
        "This document outlines the **V11.0 \"HPC-SDG\" Build & Deployment Certification Checklist**, serving as a definitive guide for the V11.0 suite. It codifies non-negotiable architectural mandates, physics implementations, and operational standards required to stabilize the platform and resolve issues from the V10.x campaign.\n",
        "\n",
        "#### 1.0 Foundational Architectural Integrity\n",
        "This section focuses on resolving V10.x pipeline failures and establishing a resilient computational foundation.\n",
        "\n",
        "*   **1.1 Unified Hashing Mandate:**\n",
        "    *   **Deterministic Generation:** The orchestrator's hashing function must remove non-deterministic elements (e.g., `time.time()`) and generate a deterministic hash (UUID) solely from the input parameter set.\n",
        "    *   **Central Authority Passing:** The orchestrator must explicitly pass this UUID (`--job_uuid`) to Worker (`worker_sncgl_sdg.py`) and Validator (`validation_pipeline_v11.py`) subprocesses for all I/O operations.\n",
        "*   **1.2 Decoupled Two-Layer Execution:**\n",
        "    *   **Layer 1 (JAX-Optimized HPC Core):** Exclusively for high-throughput, JAX-native physics (`aste_hunter`, S-NCGL Worker, SDG Geometric Solver).\n",
        "    *   **Layer 2 (Decoupled Secondary Analysis Suite):** All high-overhead, non-JAX, I/O-bound tasks (e.g., BSSN checking, TDA, visualization) are moved to asynchronous, post-processing components.\n",
        "*   **1.3 JAX HPC Compliance:**\n",
        "    *   **Control Flow Refactor:** Python `for` loops in core time-stepping replaced with `jax.lax.scan` for single, optimized XLA graph compilation.\n",
        "    *   **State Management:** Uses JAX-compliant pytrees (`NamedTuple`) for simulation state and parameters.\n",
        "    *   **Vectorized Operations:** Employs `jax.vmap` for spatial tensor operations to resolve `TypeError` and boost performance.\n",
        "\n",
        "#### 2.0 Physics Core Implementation & Unification\n",
        "This section verifies the successful transition from the BSSN solver to the axiomatically correct SDG solver, confirming \"Foundational Closure.\"\n",
        "\n",
        "*   **2.1 S-NCGL Axiomatic Derivation:** Implementation in `worker_sncgl_sdg.py` must be directly derived from the canonical Lagrangian density, $\\mathcal{L}_{\\text{FMIA}}$.\n",
        "*   **2.2 SDG Solver Integration:** The BSSN solver is fully replaced by the JAX-native SDG solver. The BSSN check (`validation_pipeline_bssn.py`) is demoted to an asynchronous, Layer 2 \"Classical GR Benchmark.\"\n",
        "*   **2.3 End-to-End Differentiable Simulation:** Integration of JAX-native S-NCGL and SDG solvers enables `aste_hunter` to use `jax.grad` for parameter search, leveraging emergent spacetime geometry as a fitness function.\n",
        "\n",
        "#### 3.0 Streamlined Validation & Governance\n",
        "This section focuses on a streamlined, high-speed validation pipeline for essential metrics.\n",
        "\n",
        "*   **3.1 Core Scientific & Physical Metrics:** `validation_pipeline_v11.py` calculates:\n",
        "    *   **Scientific Fidelity:** Log-Prime Sum of Squared Errors (SSE).\n",
        "    *   **Physical Order:** Phase Coherence Score (PCS).\n",
        "*   **3.2 Axiomatic Integrity Check:** Performs \"Noetherian Integrity Check\" by monitoring the conservation of \"Informational Coherence\" (using PCS as a proxy).\n",
        "*   **3.3 Data Contract Adherence:** `validation_pipeline_v11.py` correctly receives `--job_uuid`, locates `simulation_data_{job_uuid}.h5`, and writes `provenance_{job_uuid}.json`.\n",
        "\n",
        "#### 4.0 Operational Readiness & Deployment\n",
        "This section certifies the V11.0 Dynamic Control Hub, a web-based control plane.\n",
        "\n",
        "*   **4.1 Non-Blocking Control Plane:** `app.py` Flask server launches `core_engine.execute_hunt()` in a background thread, ensuring UI responsiveness.\n",
        "*   **4.2 Asynchronous Monitoring via Filesystem Watcher:** `ProvenanceWatcher` (using `watchdog`) runs in a dedicated background thread, monitoring for `provenance_*.json` files and updating `hub_status.json`.\n",
        "*   **4.3 Formal Deprecation of Legacy Components:** Officially decommissioned non-viable Celery/Dask orchestration, the BSSN solver (as a primary gate), and synchronous, monolithic analysis scripts.\n",
        "\n",
        "#### 5.0 Final Scientific Acceptance Criterion\n",
        "\n",
        "*   **5.1 Autonomous Discovery of the Log-Prime Spectral Attractor:** Success is defined by the autonomous discovery of a stable parameter regime that replicates the Log-Prime Spectral Attractor with an SSE of $\\leq 0.001$. This target is benchmarked against RhoSim SSE of 0.00087 and SPDC experimental data SSE of $\\approx 0.0015$."
      ]
    },

        "    *   **Deterministic Generation:** The orchestrator's hashing function must remove non-deterministic elements (e.g., `time.time()`) and generate a deterministic hash (UUID) solely from the input parameter set.\n",
        "    *   **Central Authority Passing:** The orchestrator must explicitly pass this UUID (`--job_uuid`) to Worker (`worker_sncgl_sdg.py`) and Validator (`validation_pipeline_v11.py`) subprocesses for all I/O operations.\n",

        "*   **3.2 Axiomatic Integrity Check:** Performs \"Noetherian Integrity Check\" by monitoring the conservation of \"Informational Coherence\" (using PCS as a proxy).\n",

        "## Overlapping Concerns and Dependencies: V11 Ecosystem\n",
        "\n",
        "Analyzing the summaries of the 'V11 Ecosystem Consolidation and Remediation Plan.txt' and 'Codex_ Create V11.0 HPC-SDG certification checklist suggestions - Copy.txt' reveals significant alignment, indicating a well-defined and coherent approach to the V11.0 development.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Overlapping Themes and Concepts:\n",
        "Both documents consistently emphasize the following core themes:\n",
        "1.  **Unified Hashing Mandate:** Centralized, deterministic job identification (`hashlib.sha1`) is a critical component of both the remediation plan and the certification criteria.\n",
        "2.  **JAX-HPC Compliance:** The necessity of JAX-native code, `jax.lax.scan` for control flow, and proper state management for performance and stability is a recurring mandate.\n",
        "3.  **SDG Solver Integration:** The replacement of the BSSN solver with the JAX-native Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) solver is a foundational shift addressed in both.\n",
        "4.  **Decoupled Architecture (Three-Plane/Two-Layer):** The move to an asynchronous, event-driven architecture with distinct control, data, and analysis planes (or layers) is a key architectural strategy.\n",
        "5.  **Data Contract Adherence:** Ensuring consistent and immutable data interfaces between components (e.g., via `settings.py` and canonical keys) is crucial for system integrity.\n",
        "6.  **"
      ]

      "source": [
        "## Overlapping Concerns and Dependencies: V11 Ecosystem\n",
        "\n",
        "Analyzing the summaries of the 'V11 Ecosystem Consolidation and Remediation Plan.txt' and 'Codex_ Create V11.0 HPC-SDG certification checklist suggestions - Copy.txt' reveals significant alignment, indicating a well-defined and coherent approach to the V11.0 development.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Overlapping Themes and Concepts:\n",
        "Both documents consistently emphasize the following core themes:\n",
        "1.  **Unified Hashing Mandate:** Centralized, deterministic job identification (`hashlib.sha1`) is a critical component of both the remediation plan and the certification criteria.\n",
        "2.  **JAX-HPC Compliance:** The necessity of JAX-native code, `jax.lax.scan` for control flow, and proper state management for performance and stability is a recurring mandate.\n",
        "3.  **SDG Solver Integration:** The replacement of the BSSN solver with the JAX-native Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) solver is a foundational shift addressed in both.\n",
        "4.  **Decoupled Architecture (Three-Plane/Two-Layer):** The move to an asynchronous, event-driven architecture with distinct control, data, and analysis planes (or layers) is a key architectural strategy.\n",
        "5.  **Data Contract Adherence:** Ensuring consistent and immutable data interfaces between components (e.g., via `settings.py` and canonical keys) is crucial for system integrity.\n",
        "6.  **Audit Integrity ('Trust but Verify'):** The principle that the validator must independently re-calculate metrics from raw data is upheld in both, verifying robust data provenance.\n",
        "7.  **Falsifiability-Driven Fitness:** The concept of calculating fitness based not just on SSE but also on its distinction from null models is a shared evolutionary algorithm principle.\n",
        "8.  **Deprecation of Legacy Components:** Both documents explicitly address the formal decommissioning of non-viable V10.x components like Celery/Dask orchestration and the BSSN solver as a primary gate.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Explicit Dependencies:\n",
        "Several remediation actions from the 'Consolidation and Remediation Plan' directly fulfill criteria in the 'Certification Checklist':\n",
        "*   **Unified Hashing Remediation (Plan 3.1) -> Checklist 1.1 (Unified Hashing Mandate):** The plan's ratification of `hashlib` (Variant A) and its implementation in `core_engine.py` directly addresses the checklist's requirement for deterministic generation and central authority passing of `job_uuid`.\n",
        "*   **OOM Bug Fix (Plan 3.2, Gap 1) -> Checklist 4.2 (Asynchronous Monitoring):** The removal of the `found_files` list in `app.py` directly enhances the stability and scalability of the `ProvenanceWatcher`, which is a key part of asynchronous monitoring.\n",
        "*   **Data Contract Fix ('Magic Strings') (Plan 3.2, Gap 2) -> Checklist 3.3 (Data Contract Adherence) & 4.3 (UI Robustness):** Implementing new API keys in `settings.py` and a `/api/get-constants` endpoint directly ensures the robustness of the data contract across Layer 1 components and improves the UI's operational readiness by eliminating 'magic strings'.\n",
        "*   **V11 Physics Baseline (Plan 3.3) -> Checklist 2.1 (S-NCGL Axiomatic Derivation) & 2.2 (SDG Solver Integration):** The plan's ratification of the V11 physics baseline (placeholder physics) is a prerequisite for certifying the S-NCGL axiomatic derivation and SDG solver integration, even if the full physics (V12) is a later step.\n",
        "*   **`jax.lax.scan` adoption (Plan 2.2) -> Checklist 1.3 (JAX HPC Compliance):** The implementation of `jax.lax.scan` in the worker to achieve a 'Single XLA Graph' directly satisfies the core HPC mandate for control flow refactoring.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Potential Synergistic Actions:\n",
        "1.  **Integrated Testing for Data Contracts:** Beyond mere verification, joint development of unit and integration tests that specifically assert adherence to the `settings.py` data contract would reinforce system stability and reduce 'Data Contract Drift'.\n",
        "2.  **Shared Logging & Monitoring Standards:** Establishing consistent logging formats and centralizing logging output across all planes would greatly assist in diagnosing and validating system behavior, complementing the asynchronous monitoring.\n",
        "3.  **Performance Benchmarking:** While the checklist mandates JAX HPC compliance, active collaboration between physics and engineering teams on performance benchmarking (e.g., profiling XLA graph execution) could further optimize the 'Single XLA Graph' architecture.\n",
        "4.  **Phased Physics Integration:** The plan for V12 physics injection could be systematically broken down into smaller, certifiable modules, allowing incremental validation against the 'Scientific & Physical Metrics' in the checklist during the transition.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {

        "## Outline Implementation Steps\n",
        "\n",
        "### Subtask:\n",
        "Based on the identified key objectives, checklist items, and interdependencies, outline concrete implementation steps. This step will propose actions to address the consolidation, remediation, and certification aspects mentioned in the files.\n"

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "8f294351"
      },
      "source": [
        "## Implementation Steps for V11.0 HPC-SDG Ecosystem\n",
        "\n",
        "This section outlines concrete, actionable steps to implement the V11.0 HPC-SDG ecosystem, integrating mandates from the Consolidation and Remediation Plan with the certification criteria.\n",
        "\n",
        "### 1. Unified Hashing & Identifier Management\n",
        "*   **Objective Addressed:** Resolves \"Orchestrator-Hunter Desynchronization\" deadlock; ensures reproducibility.\n",
        "*   **Actionable Steps:**\n",
        "    1.  **Refactor `core_engine.py`:** Implement `generate_deterministic_hash` using `hashlib.sha1` based on the full parameter set (Variant A). The orchestrator will be the *sole* source of `job_uuid` generation.\n",
        "    2.  **Modify CLI Arguments:** Update `worker_sncgl_sdg.py` and `validation_pipeline.py` to accept `--job_uuid` as a mandatory command-line argument, replacing any `config_hash` usage.\n",
        "    3.  **Update Artifact Naming:** Ensure `worker_sncgl_sdg.py` saves its output (`simulation_data_{job_uuid}.h5`) and `validation_pipeline.py` saves its report (`provenance_{job_uuid}.json`) using the received `job_uuid`.\n",
        "    4.  **Align `settings.py`:** Define `JOB_UUID_KEY = \"job_uuid\"` and `HASH_KEY = JOB_UUID_KEY` to formalize the canonical identifier.\n",
        "    5.  **Control Hub Watcher:** Adjust `app.py`'s `ProvenanceWatcher` to read `job_uuid` from provenance payloads using `settings.HASH_KEY`.\n",
        "\n",
        "### 2. JAX HPC Compliance & Performance Optimization\n",
        "*   **Objective Addressed:** Resolves \"JIT-out\" stall; achieves \"Single XLA Graph\" mandate.\n",
        "*   **Actionable Steps:**\n",
        "    1.  **`jax.lax.scan` Integration:** Verify `worker_sncgl_sdg.py` (or its internal solver components) explicitly uses `jax.lax.scan` for the core time-stepping loop to encapsulate the entire simulation into a single XLA graph.\n",
        "    2.  **JAX-Compliant State Management:** Confirm all dynamic simulation states and parameters (`SimState`, `FMIAState`, etc.) are managed via `NamedTuple` or other JAX-compliant pytrees within `worker_sncgl_sdg.py` to prevent `TypeError`s during JIT compilation.\n",
        "    3.  **Vectorized Operations (`jax.vmap`):** Implement `jax.vmap` for spatial tensor operations (e.g., metric inversion, derivative calculations) within `worker_sncgl_sdg.py` and `solver_sdg.py` to maximize parallelization and resolve `TypeError` issues.\n",
        "\n",
        "### 3. SDG Solver Integration & Physics Core\n",
        "*   **Objective Addressed:** Axiomatic Pivot: Decommission BSSN, commission JAX-native SDG; S-NCGL axiomatic derivation.\n",
        "*   **Actionable Steps:**\n",
        "    1.  **V11 Physics Baseline Implementation:** Ensure `solver_sdg.py` contains the specified V11 placeholder physics (e.g., `calculate_informational_stress_energy`, `solve_sdg_geometry`). This will be the stable target for the V12 upgrade.\n",
        "    2.  **Integrate `solver_sdg.py`:** Modify `worker_sncgl_sdg.py` to import and utilize functions from `solver_sdg.py` for both S-NCGL field evolution and SDG geometry calculations.\n",
        "    3.  **Demote BSSN:** Confirm that the BSSN solver (`validation_pipeline_bssn.py`) is explicitly excluded from the primary simulation loop and is relegated to a decoupled Layer 2 analysis, if used at all.\n",
        "\n",
        "### 4. Decoupled Three-Plane Architecture & Operational Readiness\n",
        "*   **Objective Addressed:** Transition to an event-driven design; non-blocking control plane; asynchronous monitoring.\n",
        "*   **Actionable Steps:**\n",
        "    1.  **Control Plane (`app.py`):** Verify `app.py`'s `/api/start-hunt` endpoint spawns `core_engine.execute_hunt()` in a *background thread* and returns an immediate HTTP 202 response.\n",
        "    2.  **Asynchronous Monitoring:** Ensure `app.py` includes a `ProvenanceWatcher` (using `watchdog`) running in a *dedicated background thread* to monitor `settings.PROVENANCE_DIR` for `provenance_*.json` files.\n",
        "    3.  **Filesystem as Message Bus:** Confirm that the Data Plane (`core_engine.py`) writes all intermediate artifacts and final provenance reports to the filesystem (`settings.DATA_DIR`, `settings.PROVENANCE_DIR`) for consumption by the Analysis Plane, without direct inter-component calls.\n",
        "    4.  **Formal Deprecation:** Explicitly remove or comment out any code related to non-viable Celery/Dask orchestration or synchronous, monolithic analysis scripts from the core codebase.\n",
        "\n",
        "### 5. Data Contract Adherence & Remediation Actions\n",
        "*   **Objective Addressed:** Eliminates \"Magic Strings\"; fixes OOM bug; formalizes data contracts.\n",
        "*   **Actionable Steps:**\n",
        "    1.  **OOM Bug Remediation:** Remove the `found_files` list and any associated `.append()` logic from `app.py`'s `update_status` function and `ProvenanceWatcher` to prevent memory leaks.\n",
        "    2.  **Centralized API Keys:** Implement `API_KEY_HUNT_STATUS`, `API_KEY_LAST_EVENT`, etc., as constants in `settings.py`.\n",
        "    3.  **Dynamic UI Contract:** Add a new `/api/get-constants` endpoint to `app.py` to serve these `API_KEY_` constants to the frontend. Update `templates/index.html`'s JavaScript to fetch these keys dynamically on page load.\n",
        "    4.  **Canonical Data Keys:** Ensure `validation_pipeline.py` (and `aste_hunter.py`) uses canonical keys from `settings.py` (e.g., `settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY`, `settings.STABILITY_METRIC_KEY`) when reading/writing provenance reports.\n",
        "\n",
        "### 6. Audit Integrity: \"Trust but Verify\"\n",
        "*   **Objective Addressed:** Ensures independent validation of simulation results.\n",
        "*   **Actionable Steps:**\n",
        "    1.  **Independent Metric Calculation:** Verify `validation_pipeline.py` loads the *raw HDF5 artifact* (`simulation_data_{job_uuid}.h5`) and independently recalculates all essential metrics (Log-Prime SSE, PCS, H-Norm) from the raw data arrays (`final_rho`, `final_g_tt`). It *must not* rely on metrics pre-calculated or stored by the worker in the HDF5 metadata (though storing them there for easy access is fine, the validator should re-derive).\n",
        "\n",
        "### 7. Final Scientific Acceptance Criterion\n",
        "*   **Objective Addressed:** Autonomous discovery of the Log-Prime Spectral Attractor.\n",
        "*   **Actionable Steps:**\n",
        "    1.  **Fitness Function:** Ensure `aste_hunter.py`'s fitness function (`process_generation_results`) accurately calculates fitness based on Log-Prime SSE and incorporates the \"Falsifiability-Driven Fitness\" bonus from the validation pipeline's null tests.\n",
        "    2.  **Termination Condition:** Implement logic within `aste_hunter.py` or `core_engine.py` to detect when a run achieves `Log-Prime SSE <= 0.001` and appropriately log this achievement, potentially triggering a success state or termination of the hunt."
      ]
    },

        "## Summary:\n",
        "\n",
        "### Data Analysis Key Findings\n",
        "\n",
        "*   **V11.0 Ecosystem Goals**: The V11.0 \"HPC-SDG\" Ecosystem aims to resolve critical scientific (\"Stability-Fidelity Paradox\") and engineering (\"Orchestrator-Hunter Desynchronization\") failures from V10.x. Key objectives include achieving \"Foundational Closure,\" ensuring Axiomatic Compliance with $\\mathcal{L}_{\\text{FMIA}}$, enabling end-to-end differentiable simulation, and ultimately discovering the Log-Prime Spectral Attractor with a Sum of Squared Errors (SSE) of $\\leq 0.001$.\n",
        "*   **Consolidation Strategies**: Proposed strategies involve an Axiomatic Pivot to a JAX-native Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) solver, a Unified Hashing Mandate using `hashlib.sha1`, a Decoupled Three-Plane Architectural Model (Control, Data, Analysis), JAX-HPC \"Single XLA Graph\" Mandate, and Centralized Configuration via `settings.py`.\n",
        "*   **Remediation Actions**: Specific actions include implementing deterministic hashing, removing the `found_files` list from `app.py` to fix OOM bugs, formalizing data contracts with API keys in `settings.py` and a `/api/get-constants` endpoint, maintaining independent auditing via `validation_pipeline.py`, ratifying the V11.0 physics baseline, and deprecating legacy components like Celery/Dask orchestration and the BSSN solver.\n",
        "*   **Certification Requirements**: The V11.0 certification checklist specifies requirements across five areas:\n",
        "    1.  **Foundational Architectural Integrity**: Emphasizes deterministic hashing (`--job_uuid`), a decoupled two-layer execution (JAX-optimized HPC core and decoupled secondary analysis), and JAX HPC compliance (`jax.lax.scan`, `NamedTuple`, `jax.vmap`).\n",
        "    2.  **Physics Core Implementation & Unification**: Focuses on S-NCGL axiomatic derivation from $\\mathcal{L}_{\\text{FMIA}}$, full replacement of BSSN with the JAX-native SDG solver, and end-to-end differentiable simulation.\n",
        "    3.  **Streamlined Validation & Governance**: Mandates calculation of Log-Prime SSE and Phase Coherence Score (PCS), Noetherian Integrity Check, and strict Data Contract Adherence.\n",
        "    4.  **Operational Readiness & Deployment**: Requires a non-blocking Control Plane (`app.py` spawning background threads), asynchronous monitoring with `ProvenanceWatcher`, and formal deprecation of legacy components.\n",
        "    5.  **Final Scientific Acceptance Criterion**: Defines success as autonomous discovery of the Log-Prime Spectral Attractor with an SSE of $\\leq 0.001$.\n",
        "*   **Key Dependencies and Synergies**: Significant overlap was identified between the remediation plan and certification checklist, with many remediation actions directly fulfilling certification criteria. For example, the unified hashing remediation directly addresses the `job_uuid` mandate in the checklist. Synergistic actions proposed include integrated testing for data contracts, shared logging standards, performance benchmarking, and phased physics integration.\n",
        "*   **Implementation Plan**: A detailed implementation plan was outlined, categorizing actionable steps into: Unified Hashing & Identifier Management, JAX HPC Compliance & Performance Optimization, SDG Solver Integration & Physics Core, Decoupled Three-Plane Architecture & Operational Readiness, Data Contract Adherence & Remediation Actions, Audit Integrity (\"Trust but Verify\"), and Final Scientific Acceptance Criterion. These steps provide a clear roadmap for V11.0 development and certification.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Insights or Next Steps\n",
        "\n",
        "*   The implementation plan leverages the strong alignment between the consolidation/remediation efforts and the certification requirements, ensuring that addressing V10.x issues directly contributes to V11.0's certificability.\n",
        "*   The next step should involve assigning specific engineering and scientific teams to each outlined implementation step, establishing clear timelines, and setting up a continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipeline with automated tests that specifically validate each certification criterion.\n"

            "\n",
            "\tMandate: Final Implementation of the V11.0 JAX-Native Co-Evolutionary Worker\n",
            "1.0 Strategic Context and Core Mandate\n",
            "This document provides the definitive technical specification for the production-ready worker_sncgl_sdg.py module. The implementation detailed herein is mandated by the project's strategic pivot away from the legacy BSSN-based architecture, which was formally invalidated during the V10.0 analysis campaign. That analysis uncovered a critical architectural vulnerability, designated the \"Geometric Crisis,\" characterized by a strong positive correlation of +0.72 between high physical order (Phase Coherence Score) and severe geometric constraint violations (H-Norm L2). This finding axiomatically falsified the BSSN solver. The pivot to the Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) solver is, therefore, not an incremental upgrade but a mandatory replacement of a component that was proven to be axiomatically incorrect for the project's unique physics.\n",
            "The core mandate for V11.0 is to rectify this foundational failure by specifying the full, end-to-end co-evolutionary loop, compiled via jax.jit. This requires the complete integration of the JAX-native SDG solver and its corresponding geometric feedback mechanism. This specification is governed by a strict \"no stubs or placeholders\" policy; all components must be production-final to enable Phase 3 System Ignition.\n",
            "2.0 S-NCGL Physics Kernel Specification\n",
            "This section defines the Sourced, Non-Local Complex Ginzburg- Landau (S-NCGL) master equation. This equation of motion serves as the foundational physics model that the worker must solve for the evolution of the complex informational field, psi.\n",
            "2.1 The S-NCGL Master Equation\n",
            "The implementation must correctly model the three core physical processes that govern the field's dynamics. These components are defined as follows:\n",
            "* Complex Diffusion: The linear term that governs the spatial spreading of informational potential across the simulation grid.\n",
            "* Nonlinear Saturation: The local, non-linear term that controls self-amplification and prevents unbounded growth. This mechanism is critical for the formation and stability of coherent, localized structures known as \"Quantules.\"\n",
            "* Non-Local \"Splash\" Term: The unique, non-local interaction that models the instantaneous redistribution of informational potential across the field following a localized collapse event. This is the primary driver of the system's complex pattern-forming dynamics.\n",
            "2.2 Non-Local Interaction Term Implementation\n",
            "The non-local \"splash\" term, which is mathematically a convolution, must be implemented with maximum computational efficiency. To meet the project's HPC requirements, this term must be calculated in Fourier space by leveraging the convolution theorem. The implementation will utilize JAX's native Fast Fourier Transform primitives, specifically jnp.fft.fftn, to transform the field and the convolution kernel, perform element-wise multiplication, and transform the result back to real space.\n",
            "The output of this S-NCGL field evolution, the Resonance Density field (rho = |psi|^2), serves as the direct physical source of emergent gravity within the co-evolutionary loop.\n",
            "3.0 The Co-Evolutionary Loop: A Step-by-Step Breakdown\n",
            "This section details the heart of the worker module: the logic for a single, discrete time-step. This entire process must be encapsulated within a pure JAX function suitable for iteration within a jax.lax.scan loop. The co-evolutionary step is broken down into three fundamental stages: Field → Source, Source → Geometry, and Geometry → Feedback.\n",
            "3.1 Stage 1 (Field → Source): The Informational Stress-Energy Tensor\n",
            "The placeholder function calculate_informational_stress_energy must be replaced with the full, axiomatically-derived physics kernel. This function serves as the bridge between the informational field and the geometric field by calculating the source of emergent gravity. The implementation must compute the Informational Stress-Energy Tensor, T^{\\text{info}}_{\\mu\\nu}, derived from the FMIA Lagrangian:\n",
            "T^{\\text{info}}_{\\mu\\nu} = \\kappa \\cdot \\rho \\cdot \\partial_\\mu \\phi \\cdot \\partial_\\nu \\phi + \\eta \\cdot \\partial_\\mu \\sqrt{\\rho} \\cdot \\partial_\\nu \\sqrt{\\rho} - g_{\\mu\\nu} \\cdot \\mathcal{L}_{\\text{FMIA}}\n",
            "For certification, this kernel must pass two non-negotiable, physics-based unit tests:\n",
            "* Tensor Symmetry Unit Test: Verifies that the output tensor is symmetric (T_{\\mu\\nu} = T_{\\nu\\mu}), a fundamental property of all physical stress-energy tensors.\n",
            "* Perfect-Fluid Reduction Test: Verifies that for a homogeneous input field (\\rho = \\text{const}), the complex tensor kernel correctly reduces to the diagonal form of a perfect fluid: diag(ρ, p, p, p).\n",
            "3.2 Stage 2 (Source → Geometry): The JAX-Native SDG Solver\n",
            "The solve_sdg_geometry stub must be replaced with the complete JAX-native Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) solver. This solver transforms the gravitational source into a new spacetime metric through a mandated two-step process:\n",
            "1. Solve Scalar PDE: The solver first solves a Poisson-like elliptic equation, \\nabla^2 \\phi = S. Here, S is a scalar source term distilled from the T_info_tensor, and the resulting scalar field phi is the Spacetime Density (\\rho_s).\n",
            "2. Compute Metric: The resulting scalar field \\rho_s is then used to algebraically compute the final metric_field (g_{\\mu\\nu}) using the project's Emergent Metric Ansatz.\n",
            "The implementation must be a JAX-native spectral solver utilizing jax.scipy.fft.fftn. The implementation must also correctly handle the critical k=0 (DC component) singularity inherent in Fourier-space solvers. This numerical procedure enforces the physical constraint that the source term must have a zero mean. The mandated solution requires a two-part procedure: (a) explicitly set the k=0 component of the transformed source term to 0.0, and (b) set the corresponding k=0 component of the Laplacian kernel to a non-zero value (e.g., 1.0) to prevent a division-by-zero error that would result in a NaN.\n",
            "3.3 Stage 3 (Geometry → Feedback): The Metric-Aware Diffusion Operator\n",
            "The apply_complex_diffusion placeholder must be replaced with the full, metric-aware covariant D'Alembertian operator (\\square_g). This operator correctly accounts for spacetime curvature when evolving the informational field, thereby \"closing the loop\" of co-evolution. The operator is derived through a three-step process:\n",
            "1. First Derivative: The covariant derivative of a scalar field \\phi is identical to its partial derivative, yielding a covector: \\nabla_\\nu \\phi = \\partial_\\nu \\phi.\n",
            "2. Second Derivative: The covariant derivative of the resulting covector introduces the geometric connection, encoded by the Christoffel symbols (\\Gamma^\\lambda_{\\mu\\nu}): \\nabla_\\mu (\\nabla_\\nu \\phi) = \\partial^2_{\\mu\\nu} \\phi - \\Gamma^\\lambda_{\\mu\\nu} \\partial_\\lambda \\phi.\n",
            "3. Final Contraction: This rank-2 tensor is contracted with the inverse metric (g^{\\mu\\nu}) to yield the final scalar operator: \\square_g \\phi = g^{\\mu\\nu} (\\partial^2_{\\mu\\nu} \\phi - \\Gamma^\\lambda_{\\mu\\nu} \\partial_\\lambda \\phi).\n",
            "The Christoffel symbols must be computed from the derivatives of the gridded metric data generated in Stage 2. The use of jax.jacfwd is explicitly forbidden for this task. The technically correct primitive for differentiating gridded data, jnp.gradient, must be used in combination with jnp.einsum for the required tensor contractions.\n",
            "These three stages constitute a single, pure JAX function, preparing the system for high-performance integration.\n",
            "4.0 HPC Architecture: Final jax.lax.scan Integration\n",
            "This section details the final assembly of the specified physics components into a single, JIT-compiled function that meets the project's high-performance computing objectives.\n",
            "4.1 Assembling the _evolve_sncgl_sdg_step Function\n",
            "The three distinct stages detailed in Section 3.0—Field → Source, Source → Geometry, and Geometry → Feedback—must be encapsulated into a single, pure JAX function. This function must be designed to adhere to the strict signature required by jax.lax.scan: (carry, x_t) -> (new_carry, y_t), where carry contains the complete simulation state at a given time step.\n",
            "4.2 Replacing the Python for Loop\n",
            "Any Python for loop in the main simulation driver must be replaced with a single call to jax.lax.scan, which will iterate the _evolve_sncgl_sdg_step function over the desired number of time steps. This architectural change is the singular solution to the performance bottlenecks that previously limited the scope of the project's parameter search. When the main driver function is decorated with @jax.jit, the JAX XLA compiler will unroll the entire scan into a single, massive, optimized computational graph. This eliminates all Python interpreter overhead during the time-stepping loop, achieving the maximum possible performance on the target accelerator hardware and enabling the high-throughput experimentation required for Phase 3.\n",
            "A performant worker must also produce well-defined, auditable outputs for downstream validation and analysis.\n",
            "5.0 Integrated Validation and Data Contracts\n",
            "The finalized worker is responsible not only for high-performance computation but also for generating the specific data artifacts required by the project's decoupled analysis and validation suites. The following data contracts are non-negotiable.\n",
            "5.1 Spectral Fidelity Contract\n",
            "The worker must be capable of saving the full spatio-temporal evolution of the Resonance Density field (rho_history). This output serves as the primary scientific record of the simulation. It must be persisted as a chunked HDF5 artifact named rho_history.h5. This file is the official input for the validation_pipeline.py script, which calculates the log-prime Sum of Squared Errors (SSE) to certify the simulation's spectral fidelity.\n",
            "5.2 Structural Validation Contract (TDA)\n",
            "The worker must generate and save a quantule_events.csv file. This file serves as the point-cloud data of simulation collapse locations and is the designated input for the tda_taxonomy_validator.py script, which performs Topological Data Analysis (TDA). The architectural justification for this output is the \"Local/Decoupled Protocol\" for TDA. This approach resolves the \"TDA BLOCKED\" constraint by separating the complex dependencies of TDA analysis from the core JAX worker environment (isolating complex, CPU-bound dependencies like ripser from the JAX/GPU worker), thereby adhering to the HPC Modularity Mandate.\n",
            "6.0 Final Implementation and Verification Checklist\n",
            "The following Markdown checklist represents the non-negotiable criteria for certifying the worker_sncgl_sdg.py module as \"production final\" and ready for Phase 3 System Ignition.\n",
            "* [ ] The solve_sdg_geometry function is implemented as a pure JAX spectral solver and correctly handles the k=0 singularity.\n",
            "* [ ] The calculate_informational_stress_energy function is implemented with the full physics kernel and passes both the Tensor Symmetry and Perfect-Fluid Reduction unit tests.\n",
            "* [ ] The covariant D'Alembertian operator is implemented, correctly computing Christoffel symbols from the metric using jnp.gradient.\n",
            "* [ ] The main simulation time loop is driven by jax.lax.scan and is fully JIT-compilable.\n",
            "* [ ] All placeholder logic, stubs (pass, ...), and mock data generators have been removed from the entire module.\n",
            "* [ ] The worker correctly generates and saves both the rho_history.h5 and quantule_events.csv artifacts as per the data contracts.\n",

            "1. Mandate and Strategic Context\n",
            "This document provides the formal technical specification for the Legacy BSSN Stability Check, a mandated analytical tool within the IRER V11.0 \"HPC-SDG\" architecture. This component was created in direct response to critical findings from the V10.x \"Long Hunt\" campaigns. Its purpose is not to validate the core physics of the V11.0 simulation loop but to serve as a persistent, classical benchmark, providing a quantitative measure of our system's deviation from classical General Relativity (GR).\n",
            "The 'Geometric Crisis' of V10.x\n",
            "The architectural pivot from V10.x to V11.0 was necessitated by the discovery of a profound incompatibility between the project's core physics and its geometric validation framework, a conflict designated the \"Geometric Crisis\". Forensic analysis of the V10.x simulation ledger revealed a strong positive correlation of +0.72 between runs with high physical coherence (measured by the Phase Coherence Score, or PCS) and those with high geometric constraint violations (measured by the Hamiltonian Norm L2).\n",
            "This \"Stability-Fidelity Paradox\" demonstrated a fundamental conflict between two core components:\n",
            "1. The Sourced, Non-Local Complex Ginzburg-Landau (S-NCGL) equation: The master equation governing the informational field dynamics.\n",
            "2. The Baumgarte-Shapiro-Shibata-Nakamura (BSSN) solver: The numerical relativity engine used to evolve the spacetime geometry according to the laws of classical General Relativity.\n",
            "The data was unequivocal: the evolutionary search for optimal, coherent physical states was actively steering the simulation into regimes that were mathematically non-compliant and numerically unstable from a classical GR perspective.\n",
            "Strategic Resolution: Demotion to a Classical Benchmark\n",
            "The formal conclusion drawn from the Geometric Crisis is that the S-NCGL dynamics source a scalar-tensor gravity, not classical GR. The V11.0 architecture resolves this crisis by replacing the BSSN solver with a JAX-native Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) solver as the primary, axiomatically correct \"law-keeper\" within the core simulation loop.\n",
            "This strategic pivot establishes the formal mandate for the Legacy BSSN Stability Check. The BSSN component is to be decoupled from the primary simulation loop and repurposed as a Layer 2 post-processor. Its sole function is to fulfill the directive to continue benchmarking new, SDG-governed solutions against the classical GR model, providing a consistent historical baseline.\n",
            "The component's modern architectural role can only be understood within this new, decoupled V11.0 ecosystem.\n",
            "--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
            "2. Architectural Role in the V11.0 Ecosystem\n",
            "The V11.0 architecture is built on a decoupled, two-layer design. This separation is of strategic importance, as it isolates the core, high-performance physics simulation from high-overhead scientific analysis. This design maximizes High-Performance Computing (HPC) throughput by preventing analytical bottlenecks from stalling the primary simulation pipeline.\n",
            "* Layer 1 (HPC Core): This is the primary, JAX-native co-evolutionary loop. It integrates the S-NCGL physics engine (worker_sncgl.py) with the SDG geometric law-keeper (solver_sdg.py). This layer is the single source of truth for the project's predictive scientific theory and is optimized exclusively for performance on hardware accelerators (GPUs/TPUs).\n",
            "* Layer 2 (Decoupled Post-Processing): This is a suite of analytical tools that run asynchronously after a Layer 1 simulation is complete. These tools perform complex, CPU-bound scientific analysis on the data artifacts produced by the HPC Core. The primary components of this layer are the Legacy BSSN Check (run_bssn_check.py) and the TDA/Quantule Analyzer (run_tda_analysis.py).\n",

            "3. Functional Specification\n",
            "This section details the precise operational mechanics of the run_bssn_check.py script, including its data contracts and the core computational logic it executes.\n",
            "Data Contracts\n",
            "The script operates on a strict, artifact-based data contract, ensuring auditable and reproducible analysis.\n",
            "* Inputs:\n",
            "   * The script requires the UUID of a completed Layer 1 simulation run (e.g., received as a --config_hash command-line argument).\n",
            "   * It uses this UUID to deterministically locate two primary artifacts in the shared data directory:\n",
            "      1. rho_history_{uuid}.h5: The HDF5 file containing the full spatio-temporal history of the Resonance Density (\\rho) field.\n",
            "      2. config_{uuid}.json: The JSON file containing the original configuration parameters for the run.\n",
            "* Outputs:\n",
            "   * The script generates a single provenance_{uuid}.json file.\n",
            "   * This artifact must contain the key metric, H_Norm_L2 (the L2 norm of the Hamiltonian constraint violation), alongside the original run's UUID to ensure unambiguous and auditable data lineage.\n",
            "Core Logic\n",
            "The script executes the following sequence of operations:\n",
            "1. Artifact Ingestion: The script receives the run UUID, constructs the full file paths to the rho_history and config artifacts, and loads them into memory.\n",
            "2. Source Term Calculation: It calculates the Informational Stress-Energy Tensor (T^{\\text{info}}_{\\mu\\nu}) from the loaded rho field data. This tensor serves as the source term that drives the evolution of the BSSN equations.\n",
            "3. BSSN Solver Execution: It executes the legacy BSSN solver, using the computed T^{\\text{info}}_{\\mu\\nu} to evolve a classical spacetime geometry from an initially flat state.\n",
            "4. Constraint Violation Monitoring: Throughout the BSSN evolution, the script continuously calculates the Hamiltonian constraint violation, which measures how far the evolved geometry deviates from a valid solution to Einstein's field equations.\n",
            "5. Metric Aggregation and Serialization: Upon completion of the BSSN evolution, the script computes the final L2 norm of the Hamiltonian constraint violation across the spatial grid. This final scalar value, H_Norm_L2, is written to the provenance_{uuid}.json artifact.\n",
            "The specific meaning and utility of the generated H_Norm_L2 metric will now be explained.\n",
            "--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
            "4. Interpretation of Results and Use Cases\n",
            "The architectural pivot to V11.0 requires a corresponding paradigm shift in how the BSSN check's output is interpreted. In the V10.x architecture, a high H_Norm_L2 was considered a \"simulation failure.\" In the V11.0 architecture, it is understood to be an expected quantitative measurement of the S-NCGL solution's deviation from classical General Relativity.\n",
            "The sole purpose of this tool is to provide comparative data for solutions that have already been deemed valid by the primary SDG solver. For any given low-SSE, high-PCS run, this tool answers the question: \"By how much does this physically coherent informational state violate the constraints of classical GR?\"\n",
            "The following table provides clear examples of how to interpret the results.\n",
            "Input Run Characteristics (SDG-Valid)\n",
            "\tLegacy BSSN Output (H_Norm_L2)\n",
            "\tAnalytical Interpretation\n",
            "\tHigh physical coherence (High PCS), Low scientific error (Low SSE)\n",
            "\tHigh\n",
            "\tThis is the expected result and validates the findings of the \"Geometric Crisis.\" It demonstrates that highly ordered S-NCGL states source a scalar-tensor gravity that is fundamentally non-compliant with classical GR.\n",
            "\tLow physical coherence (Low PCS), High scientific error (High SSE)\n",
            "\tLow\n",
            "\tThis outcome indicates that a chaotic, noise-like rho field provides a source term that is statistically close to a vacuum or trivial state in GR, thus producing low constraint violations.\n",
            "\tWith the analytical purpose of the H_Norm_L2 metric now clear, the final section details the component's formal implementation status.\n",

            "1.0 Introduction: Beyond Simulation to a Verifiable Discovery Engine\n",

            "2.0 The Analysis Layer: A Multi-Modal Validation Framework\n",
            "The Analysis Layer functions as the sensory apparatus for the entire computational ecosystem. This layer is the system's epistemological core, transforming raw numerical output into verifiable knowledge that serves as the input for the Control Layer's nervous system.\n",
            "2.1 Core Hypothesis Validation: Spectral Fidelity and Falsifiability\n",
            "The primary scientific validation target of the IRER framework is the Log-Prime Spectral Attractor Hypothesis, which predicts that stable emergent structures will exhibit spatial frequencies corresponding to the natural logarithms of prime numbers (k \\approx \\ln(p)).\n",
            "The fidelity of a simulation run against this hypothesis is quantified using the Sum of Squared Errors (SSE) as the key metric. This quantitative measure has been refined to exceptional levels of precision, with benchmarks including an aggregate directional fit of SSE ≈ 0.02 and an optimal best-run fit of SSE ≈ 0.00087.\n",
            "Scientific integrity is enforced by a Falsifiability-Driven Fitness Heuristic, a mechanism designed not merely to check results but to actively shape the fitness landscape for the Hunter AI. It calculates a \"Falsifiability Bonus\" by comparing the signal SSE against the high sentinel codes (e.g., 999.0 and 996.0) from null tests like phase scrambling. This creates steep \"cliffs\" around statistical noise, ensuring the evolutionary algorithm does not get trapped in local optima of plausible but physically meaningless results and instead converges on genuine emergent resonance.\n",
            "2.2 Stability Management: The Aletheia Coherence Metrics (ACMs)\n",
            "The central architectural trade-off the ecosystem is engineered to manage is the Stability-Fidelity Paradox. Empirical data reveals a strong positive correlation of +0.72 between the Phase Coherence Score (PCS), a measure of scientific order, and the Hamiltonian constraint violation (H-Norm L2), a measure of numerical instability. This paradox demonstrates that the most scientifically significant simulation runs are often the most numerically fragile.\n",
            "The Aletheia Coherence Metrics (ACMs) are the system's primary diagnostic tools for managing this paradox, providing an actionable, gradient-based fitness landscape for the ASTE's multi-objective optimization.\n",
            "* Phase Coherence Score (PCS): Functioning as an analogue to a superfluid order parameter, the PCS quantifies the field's smoothness and long-range order. It is calculated by measuring the spatial coherence between two parallel, sampled 1D rays using the magnitude-squared coherence function from scipy.signal.\n",
            "* Principled Localization Index (PLI): Using an implementation analogous to the Inverse Participation Ratio (IPR, calculated as IPR = sum(psi^4) / (sum(psi^2))^2), the PLI quantifies the field's tendency toward concentration (stable, localized structures) versus diffusion (a precursor to non-viable results).\n",
            "* Informational Compressibility (IC): This metric measures the field's numerical robustness. Maximizing its inverse (1/IC) provides the crucial stability gradient that the Hunter AI requires to navigate away from chaotic boundaries that produce useless sentinel codes (e.g., SSE > 900.0).\n",
            "2.3 Geometric and Structural Integrity Verification\n",
            "Beyond spectral and stability analysis, the framework mandates rigorous verification of the geometric and structural integrity of the simulation's output through a set of non-negotiable V&V protocols.\n",

The code is iteratively rejected until it receives a final STATUS: APPROVED, formalizing software creation as a governed, verifiable control loop.\n",

            "1.0 Strategic Mandate and Architectural Rationale\n",
            "The strategic importance of the V11.0 architecture lies in its deliberate departure from previous monolithic and tightly-coupled systems, which were prone to cascading failures and difficult to debug. The project has pivoted toward a lightweight, Python-native stack that prioritizes modularity, resilience, and observability. This shift is not a technological downgrade but a strategic realignment toward a \"bare-metal\" operational model. This model is more robust, transparent, and fit-for-purpose, providing the stable foundation required to conduct the project's core mission: the autonomous parametric search for critical resonance. This section codifies the core principles of this new architecture.\n",
            "1.1 Ratification of the V11.0 \"Web-Based Control Plane\"\n",
            "The \"Web-Based Control Plane\" is hereby formally ratified as the standard architecture for the IRER V11.0 suite. Its design is defined by a lightweight, robust, and directly scalable nature, capable of operating identically whether deployed in a single Google Colab instance or a multi-VM cloud environment. This approach provides a persistent meta-orchestration layer that definitively resolves prior architectural failures, such as the \"A-B-A Desynchronization\" pipeline stalls and \"Blocking Server\" issues that crippled previous versions. The control plane acts as a stable, non-blocking hub for initiating and monitoring simulation campaigns.\n",
            "1.2 The \"Hunter-Worker-Profiler\" Decoupling Principle\n",
            "The foundational philosophy of the V11.0 suite is the \"Hunter-Worker-Profiler Decoupling Principle.\" This is a deliberate design choice that mandates the strict functional and logical separation of the system's three primary components:\n",
            "* The Hunter (aste_hunter.py): The high-level adaptive learning and heuristic search engine. This component is responsible for evolutionary logic, fitness calculation, and breeding new generations of simulation parameters.\n",
            "* The Worker (worker_sncgl_sdg.py): The low-level, computationally intensive physics engine. This JAX-native kernel is responsible for executing a single S-NCGL + SDG simulation on accelerator hardware (GPU/TPU).\n",
            "* The Profiler (validation_pipeline.py): The post-hoc scientific analysis module. This component ingests the raw data artifacts from the Worker and calculates the definitive, scientifically valid metrics required by the Hunter.\n",
            "This principle is the physical implementation of the project's \"HPC Modularity and Scalability Mandate,\" ensuring that engineering flaws are isolated from scientific problems and that each component can be scaled and maintained independently.\n",
            "1.3 System Data Flow\n",
            "The interaction between the decoupled components is governed by a well-defined, asynchronous, file-based data flow, ensuring resilience and scalability. The process unfolds in three distinct stages:\n",
            "1. User Interaction: The process begins in the browser. A user accesses the index.html user interface, configures a simulation campaign (e.g., number of generations, population size), and clicks the \"Start Hunt\" button. This action triggers an asynchronous fetch call to the /api/start-hunt endpoint on the app.py control plane.\n",
            "2. Control Plane Orchestration: Upon receiving the API request, the Flask server (app.py) immediately spawns a new background thread to execute the core_engine.execute_hunt() function. This prevents the primary server process from blocking, allowing the UI to remain responsive. Concurrently, a persistent \"Watcher Thread\" within app.py continuously monitors the provenance_reports/ directory for new provenance.json artifacts. When a new report is detected, the watcher reads its contents and updates the central status.json file, which is polled by the front-end to provide real-time updates.\n",
            "3. HPC Core Execution: The background core_engine thread manages the primary evolutionary loop. It dispatches individual simulation jobs by invoking the worker_sncgl_sdg.py script with a unique configuration file. The Worker executes the JAX-native physics simulation and saves its primary output, a multi-dimensional HDF5 data file, to the simulation_data/ directory. This action subsequently triggers the validation_pipeline.py script, which loads the HDF5 artifact, calculates the final scientific metrics, and writes the definitive provenance_{uuid}.json report. The creation of this file is detected by the Control Plane's Watcher Thread, completing the feedback loop.\n",
            "This decoupled, file-based workflow ensures that the failure of any single Worker job does not crash the entire orchestration loop. The system can continue processing successful runs, making it exceptionally resilient for large-scale, autonomous discovery campaigns.\n",
            "2.0 Foundational Configuration\n",
            "In a decoupled micro-service architecture, a centralized and version-controlled configuration is critical for ensuring operational consistency. The components of the V11.0 suite rely on a shared understanding of file paths, script names, and metric keys to function as a cohesive system. This section provides the foundational dependency and configuration files that serve as this single source of truth.\n",
            "2.1 System Dependencies (requirements.txt)\n",
            "The following core Python dependencies are required for the V11.0 suite to function correctly. These packages provide the necessary functionality for the web server, file system monitoring, data handling, and high-performance computation.\n",
            "* flask\n",
            "* watchdog\n",
            "* h5py\n",
            "* JAX\n",
            "* NumPy\n",
            "* scipy\n",
            "2.2 Central Configuration (settings.py)\n",
            "The settings.py file consolidates all critical file paths, directory names, and metric keys into a single, importable module. This approach eliminates hardcoded paths within the individual components, simplifying system-wide modifications and ensuring that all modules operate from a unified configuration. Critically, settings.HASH_KEY is defined as the non-negotiable, system-wide key for job identification; all components must import and use this variable to ensure a coherent data flow contract.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "settings.py\n",
            "CLASSIFICATION: Central Configuration (IRER V11.0)\n",
            "GOAL: Consolidates all file paths, script names, and metric keys\n",
            "      for use by the entire V11.0 suite.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "import os\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Directory layout ---\n",
            "BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\n",
            "CONFIG_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"input_configs\")\n",
            "DATA_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"simulation_data\")\n",
            "PROVENANCE_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"provenance_reports\")\n",
            "LOG_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"logs\")\n",
            "LEDGER_FILE = os.path.join(LOG_DIR, \"aste_hunt_ledger.csv\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Key Metric Identifiers ---\n",
            "# These keys are the formal \"data contract\" between the Profiler and the Hunter.\n",
            "SSE_METRIC_KEY = \"log_prime_sse\"\n",
            "STABILITY_METRIC_KEY = \"sdg_h_norm_l2\" # For geometric stability\n",
            "HASH_KEY = \"job_uuid\"\n",
            "3.0 Component Implementation: The Decoupled Toolkit\n",
            "This section provides the definitive, build-ready code and a detailed functional analysis for each of the six core software components that comprise the IRER V11.0 suite. When operating in concert and governed by the central configuration, these modules constitute the complete Layer 1 validation system, from the user-facing web interface to the low-level HPC physics execution.\n",
            "3.1 The Hunter (aste_hunter.py): Adaptive Learning Engine\n",
            "1. Classification and Goal: This component is officially classified as the \"Adaptive Learning Engine.\" Its goal is to act as the \"Brain\" of the Adaptive Simulation Steering Engine (ASTE), calculating a falsifiability-driven fitness score and breeding new generations of simulation parameters.\n",
            "2. Functional Analysis: The core logic resides within the process_generation_results method. This function calculates a base_fitness derived from the inverse of the Sum of Squared Errors (SSE), rewarding solutions that more closely match the theoretical target. This base score is then augmented with a \"Falsifiability Bonus.\" This bonus, governed by the LAMBDA_FALSIFIABILITY coefficient, enforces scientific integrity by rewarding solutions that are robustly distinguishable from noise. It is calculated from the difference between the primary SSE and the SSE generated from two null-hypothesis tests: one with scrambled signal phases (_null_a_phase_scramble) and another with shuffled targets (_null_b_target_shuffle). This ensures the evolutionary search prioritizes solutions that are not only accurate but also non-trivial and scientifically meaningful.\n",
            "3. Build-Ready Code:\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "aste_hunter.py\n",
            "CLASSIFICATION: Adaptive Learning Engine (ASTE V11.0 - S-NCGL Compliant)\n",
            "GOAL: Acts as the \"Brain\" of the ASTE. Calculates fitness based on the\n",
            "      Falsifiability Bonus and breeds new generations for the S-NCGL model.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import csv\n",
            "import random\n",
            "import numpy as np\n",
            "from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional\n",
            "import sys\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Import Shared Components ---\n",
            "try:\n",
            "    import settings\n",
            "except ImportError:\n",
            "    print(\"FATAL: 'settings.py' not found. Please create it first.\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
            "    sys.exit(1)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# Configuration from centralized settings\n",
            "LEDGER_FILENAME = settings.LEDGER_FILE\n",
            "PROVENANCE_DIR = settings.PROVENANCE_DIR\n",
            "SSE_METRIC_KEY = settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY\n",
            "HASH_KEY = settings.HASH_KEY # V11 Mandate: Use central hash key\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# Evolutionary Algorithm Parameters\n",
            "TOURNAMENT_SIZE = 3\n",
            "MUTATION_RATE = 0.2\n",
            "MUTATION_STRENGTH = 0.1\n",
            "LAMBDA_FALSIFIABILITY = 0.1\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "class Hunter:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Manages population, calculates fitness, and breeds new generations.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    def __init__(self, ledger_file: str):\n",
            "        self.ledger_file = ledger_file\n",
            "        self.population = self._load_ledger()\n",
            "        self.generation = self.get_current_generation()\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def get_current_generation(self) -> int:\n",
            "        if not self.population:\n",
            "            return 0\n",
