            "    sim_params = params.get(\"simulation\", {})\n",
            "    N = sim_params.get(\"N_grid\", 64)\n",
            "    T_steps = sim_params.get(\"T_steps\", 200)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # Initialize fields\n",
            "    psi_initial = jax.random.normal(key, (N, N), dtype=jnp.complex64) * 0.1\n",
            "    rho_s_initial = jnp.ones((N, N)) * params.get(\"sdg_rho_vac\", 1.0)\n",
            "    eta_mu_nu = jnp.diag(jnp.array([-1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0]))\n",
            "    g_mu_nu_initial = jnp.tile(eta_mu_nu[:, :, None, None], (1, 1, N, N))\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    initial_state = SimState(psi_field=psi_initial, metric_field=g_mu_nu_initial, rho_s_field=rho_s_initial, step=0)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # Run the main time-stepping loop using jax.lax.scan for performance\n",
            "    scan_fn = partial(_evolve_sncgl_step, params=params)\n",
            "    final_state, psi_history = scan(scan_fn, initial_state, None, length=T_steps)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- Save final artifacts to HDF5 ---\n",
            "    output_path = os.path.join(settings.DATA_DIR, f\"rho_history_{job_uuid}.h5\")\n",
            "    with h5py.File(output_path, 'w') as f:\n",
            "        f.attrs['job_uuid'] = job_uuid\n",
            "        f.attrs['global_seed'] = params.get('global_seed', -1)\n",
            "        # Convert JAX arrays to NumPy for saving\n",
            "        f.create_dataset('final_psi_field', data=np.array(final_state.psi_field))\n",
            "        f.create_dataset('psi_history', data=np.array(psi_history), chunks=True)\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Saved HDF5 artifact to {output_path}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def main():\n",
            "    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"S-NCGL+SDG Production Worker (V11.0)\")\n",
            "    parser.add_argument(\"--params\", required=True, help=\"Path to the config_{job_uuid}.json file\")\n",
            "    parser.add_argument(\"--job_uuid\", required=True, help=\"The unique UUID for this job.\")\n",
            "    args = parser.parse_args()\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Worker [{args.job_uuid[:8]}] starting job.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        with open(args.params, 'r') as f:\n",
            "            params = json.load(f)\n",
            "    except FileNotFoundError:\n",
            "        log.error(f\"Params file not found: {args.params}\")\n",
            "        sys.exit(1)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # Initialize JAX PRNG key from the seed for reproducibility\n",
            "    seed = params.get('global_seed', int(time.time()))\n",
            "    jax_key = jax.random.PRNGKey(seed)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    run_sncgl_sdg_simulation_production(params, args.job_uuid, jax_key)\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Worker [{args.job_uuid[:8]}] work complete.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
            "    main()\n",
            "4.3. Concluding Transition\n",
            "With the primary data-producing component now specified, the next section details the decoupled validation pipeline responsible for analyzing its output artifacts.\n",
            "5. Decoupled Validator: validation_pipeline.py\n",
            "5.1. Context and Strategic Importance\n",
            "This script represents the culmination of the Phase 3 architectural decoupling, which separates heavy, post-simulation analysis from the core physics execution. This separation enables a critical \"hybrid CPU/GPU workflow,\" preventing expensive GPU resources from sitting \"100% idle\" during the CPU-bound analysis phase. Its sole purpose is to receive a job_uuid, deterministically locate the corresponding HDF5 artifact, calculate core metrics, and serialize the results into a provenance.json file. This file serves as the immutable data contract that informs the rest of the orchestration and analysis system.\n",
            "5.2. Build Instructions\n",
            "The following is the complete build-ready code for validation_pipeline.py.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "validation_pipeline.py\n",
            "CLASSIFICATION: Decoupled Validator (IRER V11.0 Phase 3)\n",
            "GOAL: Receives a UUID, deterministically finds the worker's output artifact,\n",
            "      calculates core metrics, and writes the critical provenance.json file.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "import argparse\n",
            "import time\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import h5py\n",
            "import numpy as np\n",
            "import sys\n",
            "import settings\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "log = settings.logging.getLogger(__name__)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Metric Functions (Placeholders for V11.0) ---\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def calculate_log_prime_sse(psi_history: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Core Metric: Calculates Sum of Squared Errors (SSE) against the\n",
            "    Log-Prime Spectral Attractor hypothesis (k ~ ln(p)).\n",
            "    \n",
            "    V11.0 PLACEHOLDER. The full implementation requires a Multi-Ray Directional\n",
            "    Sampling protocol to perform spectral analysis and match peaks against\n",
            "    the k ~ ln(p) attractor.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    # Placeholder: Use variance of the final state as a proxy for spectral \"noise\".\n",
            "    final_state = psi_history[-1]\n",
            "    variance = np.var(np.abs(final_state))\n",
            "    # Scale to a plausible SSE range.\n",
            "    return float(1000 * variance)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def calculate_sdg_h_norm(psi_history: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Core Metric: Placeholder for the H-Norm L2 geometric stability metric.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    # Placeholder: Use the magnitude of the final field as a proxy for stability.\n",
            "    # A large field magnitude might indicate numerical instability.\n",
            "    h_norm_proxy = np.mean(np.abs(psi_history[-1]))\n",
            "    return float(h_norm_proxy)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def main():\n",
            "    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"IRER V11.0 Validation Pipeline.\")\n",
            "    parser.add_argument(\"--job_uuid\", required=True, help=\"The UUID of the job to validate.\")\n",
            "    args = parser.parse_args()\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Validator [{args.job_uuid[:8]}] starting validation.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- 1. Artifact Retrieval (Unified Hashing Mandate Verification) ---\n",
            "    artifact_path = os.path.join(settings.DATA_DIR, f\"rho_history_{args.job_uuid}.h5\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        with h5py.File(artifact_path, 'r') as f:\n",
            "            psi_history = f['psi_history'][:]\n",
            "        log.info(f\"Successfully loaded artifact: {artifact_path}\")\n",
            "    except FileNotFoundError:\n",
            "        log.error(f\"V11.0 DEADLOCK FAILURE: Artifact not found for UUID {args.job_uuid} at {artifact_path}\")\n",
            "        sys.exit(1)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- 2. Core Metrics Calculation ---\n",
            "    sse_metric = calculate_log_prime_sse(psi_history)\n",
            "    h_norm_metric = calculate_sdg_h_norm(psi_history)\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Metrics calculated: SSE={sse_metric:.4f}, H-Norm={h_norm_metric:.4f}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- 3. Provenance File Creation & Serialization ---\n",
            "    payload = {\n",
            "        settings.HASH_KEY: args.job_uuid,\n",
            "        \"metrics\": {\n",
            "            settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY: sse_metric,\n",
            "            settings.STABILITY_METRIC_KEY: h_norm_metric\n",
            "        },\n",
            "        \"timestamp\": time.time()\n",
            "    }\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    output_path = os.path.join(settings.PROVENANCE_DIR, f\"provenance_{args.job_uuid}.json\")\n",
            "    with open(output_path, 'w') as f:\n",
            "        json.dump(payload, f, indent=2)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Provenance artifact saved: {output_path}\")\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Validator [{args.job_uuid[:8]}] validation complete.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
            "    main()\n",
            "5.3. Concluding Transition\n",
            "With the mechanism for generating and validating individual simulation runs now defined, the next component to specify is the \"brain\" of the operation: the Hunter AI that drives the evolutionary search for optimal parameters.\n",
            "6. Evolutionary AI: aste_hunter.py\n",
            "6.1. Context and Strategic Importance\n",
            "This script implements the \"Brain\" of the Adaptive Simulation & Taxonomy Engine (ASTE). Its function is to manage the complete evolutionary lifecycle of a parameter search. It operates by reading the ledger of past runs to understand historical performance, generating new parameter sets for the next generation of simulations, and processing the incoming provenance.json reports to calculate fitness scores and guide the ongoing search toward scientifically valuable regions of the parameter space.\n",
            "6.2. Build Instructions\n",
            "The following is the complete build-ready code for aste_hunter.py.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "aste_hunter.py\n",
            "CLASSIFICATION: Evolutionary AI (IRER V11.0)\n",
            "GOAL: Acts as the \"Brain\" of the ASTE. It reads validation reports,\n",
            "      calculates fitness, and breeds new generations of parameters to\n",
            "      guide the autonomous parameter search.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import csv\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import random\n",
            "from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional\n",
            "import settings\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "log = settings.logging.getLogger(__name__)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "class Hunter:\n",
            "    \"\"\"Encapsulates the evolutionary logic for the ASTE.\"\"\"\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def __init__(self, ledger_file: str):\n",
            "        self.ledger_file = ledger_file\n",
            "        self.fieldnames = [settings.HASH_KEY, \"generation\", \"param_kappa\", \"param_sigma_k\",\n",
            "                           settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY, settings.STABILITY_METRIC_KEY, \"fitness\"]\n",
            "        self.population = self._load_ledger()\n",
            "        log.info(f\"Hunter initialized with {len(self.population)} runs from ledger.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def _load_ledger(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
            "        \"\"\"Loads and parses the existing CSV ledger file.\"\"\"\n",
            "        if not os.path.exists(self.ledger_file):\n",
            "            return []\n",
            "        try:\n",
            "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'r', newline='') as f:\n",
            "                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n",
            "                return [dict(row) for row in reader]\n",
            "        except Exception as e:\n",
            "            log.error(f\"Failed to load ledger {self.ledger_file}: {e}\")\n",
            "            return []\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def _save_ledger(self):\n",
            "        \"\"\"Saves the current population state to the CSV ledger.\"\"\"\n",
            "        try:\n",
            "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'w', newline='') as f:\n",
            "                writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=self.fieldnames)\n",
            "                writer.writeheader()\n",
            "                writer.writerows(self.population)\n",
            "        except Exception as e:\n",
            "            log.error(f\"Failed to save ledger {self.ledger_file}: {e}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def get_current_generation(self) -> int:\n",
            "        \"\"\"Gets the next generation number to run.\"\"\"\n",
            "        if not self.population:\n",
            "            return 0\n",
            "        max_gen = 0\n",
            "        for item in self.population:\n",
            "            try:\n",
            "                gen = int(float(item.get(\"generation\", 0)))\n",
            "                if gen > max_gen:\n",
            "                    max_gen = gen\n",
            "            except (ValueError, TypeError):\n",
            "                continue # Skip malformed rows\n",
            "        return max_gen + 1\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def get_next_generation(self, population_size: int) -> List[Dict[str, float]]:\n",
            "        \"\"\"\n",
            "        Generates a new set of parameters using simple random generation for V11.0.\n",
            "        \"\"\"\n",
            "        generation: List[Dict[str, float]] = []\n",
            "        for _ in range(population_size):\n",
            "            generation.append({\n",
            "                \"param_kappa\": round(random.uniform(0.05, 0.5), 4),\n",
            "                \"param_sigma_k\": round(random.uniform(0.1, 1.0), 4),\n",
            "            })\n",
            "        return generation\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def register_new_jobs(self, new_jobs: List[Dict[str, Any]]):\n",
            "        \"\"\"Adds newly created jobs to the internal ledger before they run.\"\"\"\n",
            "        self.population.extend(new_jobs)\n",
            "        self._save_ledger()\n",
            "        log.info(f\"Registered {len(new_jobs)} new jobs in ledger.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def process_generation_results(self, completed_job_uuids: List[str]):\n",
            "        \"\"\"\n",
            "        Processes provenance reports, calculates fitness, and updates the ledger.\n",
            "        \"\"\"\n",
            "        processed_count = 0\n",
            "        pop_lookup = {run[settings.HASH_KEY]: run for run in self.population}\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        for job_uuid in completed_job_uuids:\n",
            "            prov_file = os.path.join(settings.PROVENANCE_DIR, f\"provenance_{job_uuid}.json\")\n",
            "            if not os.path.exists(prov_file):\n",
            "                log.warning(f\"Missing provenance for {job_uuid[:8]}... Skipping.\")\n",
            "                continue\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "            try:\n",
            "                with open(prov_file, 'r') as f:\n",
            "                    provenance = json.load(f)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                run_to_update = pop_lookup.get(job_uuid)\n",
            "                if not run_to_update:\n",
            "                    log.warning(f\"{job_uuid[:8]} not in population ledger. Skipping.\")\n",
            "                    continue\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                metrics = provenance.get(\"metrics\", {})\n",
            "                sse = float(metrics.get(settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY, 9999.0))\n",
            "                h_norm = float(metrics.get(settings.STABILITY_METRIC_KEY, 9999.0))\n",
            "                fitness = 0.0\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                # V11.0 Fitness Rule: if SSE is not a failure sentinel, assign fitness.\n",
            "                if sse < 900.0:\n",
            "                    fitness = 1.0 / max(sse, 1e-9)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                run_to_update.update({\n",
            "                    settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY: sse,\n",
            "                    settings.STABILITY_METRIC_KEY: h_norm,\n",
            "                    \"fitness\": fitness\n",
            "                })\n",
            "                processed_count += 1\n",
            "            except Exception as e:\n",
            "                log.error(f\"Failed to process provenance for {job_uuid[:8]}: {e}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        self._save_ledger()\n",
            "        log.info(f\"Successfully processed and updated {processed_count} runs.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def get_best_run(self) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
            "        \"\"\"Retrieves the best-performing run from the ledger.\"\"\"\n",
            "        valid_runs = [r for r in self.population if r.get('fitness') and float(r['fitness']) > 0]\n",
            "        if not valid_runs:\n",
            "            return None\n",
            "        return max(valid_runs, key=lambda r: float(r['fitness']))\n",
            "6.3. Concluding Transition\n",
            "With the Hunter AI defined, the final step in the core HPC specification is to assemble the orchestration layers that will manage the interaction between the Hunter and the Worker/Validator scripts.\n",
            "7. Core Orchestration Engine: core_engine.py\n",
            "7.1. Context and Strategic Importance\n",
            "This script contains the refactored main orchestration logic for the V11.0 suite. Its strategic role is to serve as the non-blocking engine that manages the entire hunt lifecycle, from parameter generation to final analysis. It is designed to be invoked as a background process by the app.py web server, executing the main evolutionary loop by coordinating the Hunter AI with the Worker and Validator subprocesses, thereby realizing the project's \"Hunter-Worker Decoupling Principle.\"\n",
            "7.2. Build Instructions\n",
            "The following is the complete build-ready code for core_engine.py.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "core_engine.py\n",
            "CLASSIFICATION: Core Orchestrator (IRER V11.0)\n",
            "GOAL: Manages the hunt lifecycle by coordinating the Hunter, Worker,\n",
            "      and Validator. Designed to be called by app.py as a non-blocking\n",
            "      background process.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import subprocess\n",
            "import sys\n",
            "import uuid\n",
            "import random\n",
            "import settings\n",
            "from aste_hunter import Hunter\n",
            "from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "log = settings.logging.getLogger(__name__)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def run_simulation_job(job_uuid: str, params_filepath: str) -> bool:\n",
            "    \"\"\"Executes a single end-to-end simulation job (Worker + Validator).\"\"\"\n",
            "    log.info(f\"--- [CoreEngine] STARTING JOB {job_uuid[:8]} ---\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        # --- 1. Execute Worker ---\n",
            "        worker_cmd = [sys.executable, settings.WORKER_SCRIPT, \"--params\", params_filepath, \"--job_uuid\", job_uuid]\n",
            "        log.info(f\"-> Spawning Worker: {' '.join(worker_cmd)}\")\n",
            "        subprocess.run(worker_cmd, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True)\n",
            "        log.info(f\"<- Worker {job_uuid[:8]} OK.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        # --- 2. Execute Validator ---\n",
            "        validator_cmd = [sys.executable, settings.VALIDATOR_SCRIPT, \"--job_uuid\", job_uuid]\n",
            "        log.info(f\"-> Spawning Validator: {' '.join(validator_cmd)}\")\n",
            "        subprocess.run(validator_cmd, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True)\n",
            "        log.info(f\"<- Validator {job_uuid[:8]} OK.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n",
            "        log.error(f\"JOB {job_uuid[:8]} FAILED (Exit Code {e.returncode}).\")\n",
            "        log.error(f\"  STDOUT: {e.stdout.strip()}\")\n",
            "        log.error(f\"  STDERR: {e.stderr.strip()}\")\n",
            "        return False\n",
            "    except Exception as e:\n",
            "        log.error(f\"JOB {job_uuid[:8]} crashed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n",
            "        return False\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    log.info(f\"--- [CoreEngine] JOB SUCCEEDED {job_uuid[:8]} ---\")\n",
            "    return True\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def execute_hunt(num_generations: int, population_size: int) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    The main entry point for an evolutionary hunt.\n",
            "    Returns the final 'best run' dictionary on completion.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    log.info(\"--- [CoreEngine] V11.0 HUNT EXECUTION STARTED ---\")\n",
            "    hunter = Hunter(ledger_file=settings.LEDGER_FILE)\n",
            "    start_gen = hunter.get_current_generation()\n",
            "    end_gen = start_gen + num_generations\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Executing Hunt: {num_generations} generations (from {start_gen} to {end_gen-1})\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    for gen in range(start_gen, end_gen):\n",
            "        log.info(f\"--- [CoreEngine] STARTING GENERATION {gen} ---\")\n",
            "        parameter_batch = hunter.get_next_generation(population_size)\n",
            "        jobs_to_run: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n",
            "        jobs_to_register: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        for phys_params in parameter_batch:\n",
            "            job_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n",
            "            full_params = {\n",
            "                settings.HASH_KEY: job_uuid,\n",
            "                \"global_seed\": random.randint(0, 2**32 - 1),\n",
            "                \"simulation\": {\"N_grid\": 64, \"T_steps\": 200},\n",
            "                **phys_params\n",
            "            }\n",
            "            params_filepath = os.path.join(settings.CONFIG_DIR, f\"config_{job_uuid}.json\")\n",
            "            with open(params_filepath, 'w') as f:\n",
            "                json.dump(full_params, f, indent=2)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "            jobs_to_run.append({\"job_uuid\": job_uuid, \"params_filepath\": params_filepath})\n",
            "            jobs_to_register.append({settings.HASH_KEY: job_uuid, \"generation\": gen, **phys_params})\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        hunter.register_new_jobs(jobs_to_register)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        completed_job_uuids = []\n",
            "        for job in jobs_to_run:\n",
            "            if run_simulation_job(job['job_uuid'], job['params_filepath']):\n",
            "                completed_job_uuids.append(job['job_uuid'])\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        log.info(f\"--- [CoreEngine] FINISHING GENERATION {gen} ---\")\n",
            "        hunter.process_generation_results(completed_job_uuids)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    log.info(\"--- [CoreEngine] ALL GENERATIONS COMPLETE ---\")\n",
            "    best_run = hunter.get_best_run()\n",
            "    if best_run:\n",
            "        log.info(f\"Final best run found: {json.dumps(best_run, indent=2)}\")\n",
            "    else:\n",
            "        log.warning(\"No successful runs found during the hunt.\")\n",
            "    return best_run\n",
            "7.3. Concluding Transition\n",
            "With the core engine specified, the final components of the V11.0 suite are the web server and user interface that provide dynamic, user-driven control over this powerful orchestration engine.\n",
            "8. Dynamic Control Hub: app.py\n",
            "8.1. Context and Strategic Importance\n",
            "This file specifies the \"Dynamic Control Hub,\" the persistent meta-orchestration layer for the V11.0 suite. It fulfills a dual role: first, it acts as a lightweight Flask web server to handle user requests originating from the web UI, enabling operators to launch and monitor hunts without direct command-line interaction. Second, it runs a persistent \"Watcher\" service in the background, which performs asynchronous, event-driven analysis of simulation artifacts as they are produced, providing a live, responsive view of the system's progress.\n",
            "8.2. Build Instructions\n",
            "The following is the complete build-ready code for app.py.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "app.py\n",
            "CLASSIFICATION: Meta-Orchestrator (IRER V11.0 Control Plane)\n",
            "GOAL: Runs a persistent Flask server to act as the \"Dynamic Control Hub,\"\n",
            "      providing a web UI and asynchronous artifact processing.\n",
            "\"\"\"\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import logging\n",
            "import threading\n",
            "from flask import Flask, render_template, jsonify, request\n",
            "from watchdog.observers import Observer\n",
            "from watchdog.events import FileSystemEventHandler\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import core_engine\n",
            "import settings\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Flask App Setup ---\n",
            "app = Flask(__name__, template_folder='templates')\n",
            "log = settings.logging.getLogger(__name__)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Global State & Status File ---\n",
            "g_hunt_lock = threading.Lock()\n",
            "g_hunt_in_progress = False\n",
            "STATUS_FILE = os.path.join(settings.LOG_DIR, \"status.json\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Background Watcher Service ---\n",
            "class ProvenanceWatcher(FileSystemEventHandler):\n",
            "    \"\"\"Monitors for new provenance.json files and updates central status.\"\"\"\n",
            "    def on_created(self, event):\n",
            "        if not event.is_directory and \"provenance_\" in event.src_path and event.src_path.endswith('.json'):\n",
            "            log.info(f\"Watcher: Detected new artifact: {os.path.basename(event.src_path)}\")\n",
            "            self._trigger_layer_2_analysis(event.src_path)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def _trigger_layer_2_analysis(self, prov_path: str):\n",
            "        \"\"\"Placeholder for Layer 2 analysis (TDA, etc.) and status update.\"\"\"\n",
            "        try:\n",
            "            with open(prov_path, 'r') as f:\n",
            "                prov_data = json.load(f)\n",
            "            \n",
            "            metrics = prov_data.get(\"metrics\", {})\n",
            "            current_status = {\"hunt_status\": \"Running\"}\n",
            "            if os.path.exists(STATUS_FILE):\n",
            "                with open(STATUS_FILE, 'r') as f:\n",
            "                    current_status = json.load(f)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "            # Update status with the latest event data\n",
            "            current_status[\"last_event\"] = f\"Processed {prov_data.get('job_uuid', 'N/A')[:8]}\"\n",
            "            current_status[\"last_sse\"] = metrics.get(settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY, -1)\n",
            "            current_status[\"last_h_norm\"] = metrics.get(settings.STABILITY_METRIC_KEY, -1)\n",
            "            \n",
            "            with open(STATUS_FILE, 'w') as f:\n",
            "                json.dump(current_status, f, indent=2)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        except Exception as e:\n",
            "            log.error(f\"Watcher: Failed to process artifact {prov_path}: {e}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def start_watcher_service():\n",
            "    \"\"\"Initializes and starts the filesystem watcher thread.\"\"\"\n",
            "    observer = Observer()\n",
            "    observer.schedule(ProvenanceWatcher(), settings.PROVENANCE_DIR, recursive=False)\n",
            "    observer.start()\n",
            "    log.info(f\"Watcher Service started, monitoring {settings.PROVENANCE_DIR}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- API Endpoints ---\n",
            "@app.route('/')\n",
            "def index():\n",
            "    \"\"\"Serves the main control panel UI.\"\"\"\n",
            "    return render_template('index.html')\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "@app.route('/api/start-hunt', methods=['POST'])\n",
            "def api_start_hunt():\n",
            "    \"\"\"API endpoint to start the hunt in a non-blocking background thread.\"\"\"\n",
            "    global g_hunt_in_progress\n",
            "    with g_hunt_lock:\n",
            "        if g_hunt_in_progress:\n",
            "            return jsonify({\"message\": \"A hunt is already in progress.\"}), 409\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    data = request.json or {}\n",
            "    num_generations = int(data.get('generations', 10))\n",
            "    population_size = int(data.get('population_size', 10))\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def run_hunt_in_background(gens, pop):\n",
            "        global g_hunt_in_progress\n",
            "        with g_hunt_lock:\n",
            "            g_hunt_in_progress = True\n",
            "        \n",
            "        with open(STATUS_FILE, 'w') as f:\n",
            "            json.dump({\"hunt_status\": \"Running\", \"last_event\": \"Hunt initiated...\", \"last_sse\": \"-\", \"last_h_norm\": \"-\"}, f)\n",
            "        \n",
            "        core_engine.execute_hunt(gens, pop)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        with open(STATUS_FILE, 'w') as f:\n",
            "            json.dump({\"hunt_status\": \"Idle\", \"last_event\": \"Hunt complete.\"}, f)\n",
            "        \n",
            "        with g_hunt_lock:\n",
            "            g_hunt_in_progress = False\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    hunt_thread = threading.Thread(target=run_hunt_in_background, args=(num_generations, population_size), daemon=True)\n",
            "    hunt_thread.start()\n",
            "    \n",
            "    return jsonify({\"status\": \"Hunt Started\"}), 202\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "@app.route('/api/get-status')\n",
            "def api_get_status():\n",
            "    \"\"\"API endpoint for the UI to poll for live status updates.\"\"\"\n",
            "    if not os.path.exists(STATUS_FILE):\n",
            "        return jsonify({\"hunt_status\": \"Idle\", \"last_event\": \"-\", \"last_sse\": \"-\", \"last_h_norm\": \"-\"})\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        with open(STATUS_FILE, 'r') as f:\n",
            "            return jsonify(json.load(f))\n",
            "    except Exception as e:\n",
            "        return jsonify({\"hunt_status\": f\"Error: {e}\"}), 500\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Main Application Runner ---\n",
            "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
            "    for path in [settings.CONFIG_DIR, settings.DATA_DIR, settings.PROVENANCE_DIR, settings.LOG_DIR]:\n",
            "        os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True)\n",
            "    \n",
            "    start_watcher_service()\n",
            "    \n",
            "    log.info(\"Control Hub: Starting Flask server on http://0.0.0.0:8080\")\n",
            "    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=8080)\n",
            "8.3. Concluding Transition\n",
            "The final piece of the HPC core is the user interface that interacts with the API endpoints defined in app.py, providing a complete, user-driven simulation workbench.\n",
            "9. User Interface: templates/index.html\n",
            "9.1. Context and Strategic Importance\n",
            "This HTML file provides the user-facing \"input console\" and dashboard for the entire simulation workbench. Its function is to provide a simple, intuitive interface that allows an operator to configure and launch a new evolutionary hunt and to monitor its status in real-time. This component abstracts away the complexity of the underlying command-line tools, making the powerful HPC core accessible to a broader range of users and streamlining the experimental workflow.\n",
            "9.2. Build Instructions\n",
            "The following is the complete build-ready code for index.html.\n",
            "<!DOCTYPE html>\n",
            "<html lang=\"en\">\n",
            "<head>\n",
            "    <meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n",
            "    <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\">\n",
            "    <title>IRER V11.0 Control Hub</title>\n",
            "    <style>\n",
            "        body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, \"Segoe UI\", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; background-color: #111827; color: #d1d5db; line-height: 1.5; padding: 2rem; }\n",
            "        .container { max-width: 800px; margin: auto; }\n",
            "        .card { background-color: #1f2937; padding: 1.5rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.1), 0 2px 4px -2px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); margin-bottom: 1.5rem; }\n",
            "        h1, h2 { color: #f9fafb; }\n",
            "        h1 { font-size: 2.25rem; font-weight: bold; }\n",
            "        h2 { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 1rem; }\n",
            "        label { display: block; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; font-weight: 500; color: #9ca3af; }\n",
            "        input { width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; background-color: #374151; border: 1px solid #4b5563; color: #f9fafb; padding: 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; }\n",
            "        button { width: 100%; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; background-color: #0e7490; color: white; padding: 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; border: none; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s; }\n",
            "        button:hover { background-color: #155e75; }\n",
            "        button:disabled { background-color: #4b5563; cursor: not-allowed; }\n",
            "        .hidden { display: none; }\n",
            "        .spinner { border: 4px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3); border-radius: 50%; border-top-color: #fff; width: 1rem; height: 1rem; animation: spin 1s ease-in-out infinite; margin-left: 0.5rem; }\n",
            "        @keyframes spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } }\n",
            "        #status-event, #status-sse, #status-h-norm { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: bold; color: #f9fafb; font-family: monospace; }\n",
            "    </style>\n",
            "</head>\n",
            "<body>\n",
            "    <div class=\"container\">\n",
            "        <h1>IRER V11.0 \"HPC-SDG\" Control Hub</h1>\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        <div class=\"card\">\n",
            "            <h2>HPC Core Control</h2>\n",
            "            <form id=\"hunt-form\">\n",
            "                <div style=\"margin-bottom: 1rem;\">\n",
            "                    <label for=\"generations\">Generations</label>\n",
            "                    <input type=\"number\" id=\"generations\" name=\"generations\" placeholder=\"e.g., 10\" value=\"5\">\n",
            "                </div>\n",
            "                <div>\n",
            "                    <label for=\"population\">Population Size</label>\n",
            "                    <input type=\"number\" id=\"population\" name=\"population\" placeholder=\"e.g., 20\" value=\"10\">\n",
            "                </div>\n",
            "                <button type=\"submit\" id=\"start-hunt-btn\" style=\"margin-top: 1.5rem;\">\n",
            "                    <span id=\"btn-text\">Start New Hunt</span>\n",
            "                    <div id=\"btn-spinner\" class=\"spinner hidden\"></div>\n",
            "                </button>\n",
            "            </form>\n",
            "        </div>\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        <div class=\"card\">\n",
            "            <h2>Live Hunt Status</h2>\n",
            "            <p id=\"hunt-status\" style=\"font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.1rem;\">Idle</p>\n",
            "            <div style=\"margin-top: 1rem; background-color: #374151; padding: 1rem; border-radius: 0.375rem;\">\n",
            "                <h3 style=\"color: #9ca3af; font-size: 0.875rem;\">LAST EVENT</h3>\n",
            "                <p id=\"status-event\">-</p>\n",
            "                <h3 style=\"color: #9ca3af; font-size: 0.875rem; margin-top: 1rem;\">LAST SSE</h3>\n",
            "                <p id=\"status-sse\">-</p>\n",
            "                <h3 style=\"color: #9ca3af; font-size: 0.875rem; margin-top: 1rem;\">LAST H-NORM</h3>\n",
            "                <p id=\"status-h-norm\">-</p>\n",
            "            </div>\n",
            "        </div>\n",
            "    </div>\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    <script>\n",
            "        const huntForm = document.getElementById('hunt-form');\n",
            "        const startBtn = document.getElementById('start-hunt-btn');\n",
            "        const btnText = document.getElementById('btn-text');\n",
            "        const btnSpinner = document.getElementById('btn-spinner');\n",
            "        \n",
            "        const huntStatusEl = document.getElementById('hunt-status');\n",
            "        const statusEventEl = document.getElementById('status-event');\n",
            "        const statusSseEl = document.getElementById('status-sse');\n",
            "        const statusHNormEl = document.getElementById('status-h-norm');\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        let pollingInterval = null;\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        function setButtonLoading(isLoading, text = 'Start New Hunt') {\n",
            "            startBtn.disabled = isLoading;\n",
            "            btnText.textContent = text;\n",
            "            isLoading ? btnSpinner.classList.remove('hidden') : btnSpinner.classList.add('hidden');\n",
            "        }\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        async function updateStatus() {\n",
            "            try {\n",
            "                const response = await fetch('/api/get-status');\n",
            "                const data = await response.json();\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                huntStatusEl.textContent = data.hunt_status || 'Unknown';\n",
            "                statusEventEl.textContent = data.last_event || '-';\n",
            "                statusSseEl.textContent = data.last_sse !== undefined && data.last_sse !== \"-\" ? parseFloat(data.last_sse).toFixed(4) : '-';\n",
            "                statusHNormEl.textContent = data.last_h_norm !== undefined && data.last_h_norm !== \"-\" ? parseFloat(data.last_h_norm).toFixed(4) : '-';\n",
            "                \n",
            "                if (data.hunt_status === \"Running\") {\n",
            "                    setButtonLoading(true, 'Hunt Running...');\n",
            "                    if (!pollingInterval) { // Start polling if not already running\n",
            "                        pollingInterval = setInterval(updateStatus, 3000);\n",
            "                    }\n",
            "                } else {\n",
            "                    setButtonLoading(false);\n",
            "                    if (pollingInterval) {\n",
            "                        clearInterval(pollingInterval);\n",
            "                        pollingInterval = null;\n",
            "                    }\n",
            "                }\n",
            "            } catch (error) {\n",
            "                huntStatusEl.textContent = 'Error connecting to server.';\n",
            "                console.error(\"Status poll failed:\", error);\n",
            "            }\n",
            "        }\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        huntForm.addEventListener('submit', async (event) => {\n",
            "            event.preventDefault();\n",
            "            setButtonLoading(true, 'Starting...');\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "            const formData = new FormData(huntForm);\n",
            "            const data = {\n",
            "                'generations': formData.get('generations'),\n",
            "                'population_size': formData.get('population')\n",
            "            };\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "            try {\n",
            "                const response = await fetch('/api/start-hunt', {\n",
            "                    method: 'POST',\n",
            "                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n",
            "                    body: JSON.stringify(data)\n",
            "                });\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                if (response.status === 202) {\n",
            "                    huntStatusEl.textContent = 'Hunt Started';\n",
            "                    setButtonLoading(true, 'Hunt Running...');\n",
            "                    if (!pollingInterval) {\n",
            "                        pollingInterval = setInterval(updateStatus, 3000);\n",
            "                    }\n",
            "                } else {\n",
            "                    const errorData = await response.json();\n",
            "                    huntStatusEl.textContent = errorData.message || 'Error starting hunt.';\n",
            "                    setButtonLoading(false);\n",
            "                }\n",
            "            } catch (error) {\n",
            "                huntStatusEl.textContent = 'Error: Could not connect to server.';\n",
            "                setButtonLoading(false);\n",
            "            }\n",
            "        });\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        // Initial status check on page load\n",
            "        document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', updateStatus);\n",
            "    </script>\n",
            "</body>\n",
            "</html>\n",
            "\n",
            "\tIRER V11.0 \"HPC-SDG\" Validation Suite: Complete Build Plan\n",
            "This build plan provides the complete, build-ready source code for all components of the Informational Resonance and Emergence of Reality (IRER) V11.0 \"HPC-SDG\" suite. This document codifies the project's strategic pivot to a locked, high-performance, end-to-end differentiable JAX core managed by a dynamic control hub. The implementation formalizes the decoupled \"Hunter-Worker-Validator\" architecture, a model mandated for its resilience, scalability, and capacity to distinguish between architectural and scientific failure. Each component is presented as a discrete, production-ready artifact, forming a complete system for achieving predictive autonomy in the discovery of novel physics.\n",
            "1. Phase 1: Foundational Configuration (settings.py)\n",
            "A centralized configuration file is of paramount strategic importance for any distributed computational system. The settings.py module is mandated as the single source of truth for the entire V11.0 suite, consolidating all file paths, script names, and data contract keys into one location. This practice guarantees architectural isomorphism by eliminating hard-coded values, simplifying maintenance, and ensuring that changes to the system's layout can be made in a single file without altering the logic of the individual components.\n",
            "#\n",
            "# settings.py\n",
            "# CLASSIFICATION: Central Configuration (IRER V11.0)\n",
            "# GOAL: Consolidates all file paths, script names, and metric keys\n",
            "#       for use by the entire V11.0 suite.\n",
            "#\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import os\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Directory Layout ---\n",
            "BASE_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\n",
            "CONFIG_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"input_configs\")\n",
            "DATA_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"simulation_data\")\n",
            "PROVENANCE_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"provenance_reports\")\n",
            "LOG_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"logs\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Core File Paths ---\n",
            "LEDGER_FILE = os.path.join(LOG_DIR, \"aste_hunt_ledger.csv\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Script Names (for subprocess calls) ---\n",
            "WORKER_SCRIPT = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"worker_sncgl_sdg.py\")\n",
            "VALIDATOR_SCRIPT = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, \"validation_pipeline.py\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# --- Data Contract & Ledger Keys ---\n",
            "HASH_KEY = \"job_uuid\" # The V11 unified hashing mandate\n",
            "SSE_METRIC_KEY = \"log_prime_sse\"\n",
            "STABILITY_METRIC_KEY = \"hamiltonian_norm_l2\" # Primary geometric stability metric\n",
            "With the static configuration established, we now turn to the dynamic \"brain\" of the system: the Adaptive Learning Engine that steers the scientific discovery process.\n",
            "2. Phase 2: The Adaptive Learning Engine (aste_hunter.py)\n",
            "The aste_hunter.py module is the engine of predictive autonomy, engineered to solve the \"Parameter Provenance Gap\" that plagued previous efforts. It moves the project away from \"retrofitted\" parameters toward autonomous discovery. It functions as the \"brain\" of the Adaptive Simulation Steering Engine (ASTE), managing a persistent \"gene pool\" of simulation parameters. By employing an evolutionary algorithm, the Hunter analyzes validation reports from the Validator—consuming key data such as the spectral fidelity (log_prime_sse) and the mandated Aletheia Coherence Metrics (pcs_score, pli_score)—to breed new generations of parameters. This process progressively guides the search toward parameter regimes that yield scientifically valid and numerically stable solutions.\n",
            "#\n",
            "# aste_hunter.py\n",
            "# CLASSIFICATION: Adaptive Learning Engine (ASTE V11.0)\n",
            "# GOAL: Acts as the \"Brain\" of the ASTE. It reads validation reports\n",
            "#       (provenance.json), updates its internal ledger, and applies an\n",
            "#       evolutionary algorithm to breed new parameters that optimize for\n",
            "#       low SSE and high stability.\n",
            "#\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import csv\n",
            "import random\n",
            "import sys\n",
            "import math\n",
            "from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import settings\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# Evolutionary Algorithm Parameters\n",
            "TOURNAMENT_SIZE = 3\n",
            "MUTATION_RATE = 0.15\n",
            "MUTATION_STRENGTH = 0.1\n",
            "LAMBDA_FALSIFIABILITY = 0.1\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "# V11.0 S-NCGL Parameter Space Mandate. Replaces legacy FMIA parameters.\n",
            "PARAM_SPACE = {\n",
            "    \"sigma_k\": {\"min\": 1.0, \"max\": 5.0},      # Non-local kernel width\n",
            "    \"sncgl_epsilon\": {\"min\": 0.05, \"max\": 0.5}, # Linear growth rate\n",
            "}\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "class Hunter:\n",
            "    \"\"\" Manages the evolutionary 'hunt' for optimal parameters. \"\"\"\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def __init__(self, ledger_file: str = settings.LEDGER_FILE):\n",
            "        self.ledger_file = ledger_file\n",
            "        self.fieldnames = [\n",
            "            settings.HASH_KEY, settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY, \"fitness\", \"generation\",\n",
            "            \"pcs_score\", \"pli_score\", \"ic_score\", \"hamiltonian_norm_l2\",\n",
            "            \"sse_null_phase_scramble\", \"sse_null_target_shuffle\",\n",
            "            *PARAM_SPACE.keys()\n",
            "        ]\n",
            "        self.population = self._load_ledger()\n",
            "        print(f\"[Hunter] Initialized. Loaded {len(self.population)} runs from {self.ledger_file}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def _load_ledger(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
            "        if not os.path.exists(self.ledger_file):\n",
            "            os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(self.ledger_file), exist_ok=True)\n",
            "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'w', newline='') as f:\n",
            "                writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=self.fieldnames)\n",
            "                writer.writeheader()\n",
            "            return []\n",
            "        \n",
            "        population = []\n",
            "        try:\n",
            "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'r') as f:\n",
            "                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n",
            "                for row in reader:\n",
            "                    for key in self.fieldnames:\n",
            "                        if key in row and row[key]:\n",
            "                            try:\n",
            "                                row[key] = float(row[key])\n",
            "                            except (ValueError, TypeError):\n",
            "                                pass\n",
            "                    population.append(row)\n",
            "        except Exception as e:\n",
            "            print(f\"[Hunter Error] Failed to load ledger: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
            "        return population\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def _save_ledger(self):\n",
            "        try:\n",
            "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'w', newline='') as f:\n",
            "                writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=self.fieldnames)\n",
            "                writer.writeheader()\n",
            "                writer.writerows(self.population)\n",
            "        except Exception as e:\n",
            "            print(f\"[Hunter Error] Failed to save ledger: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def get_current_generation(self) -> int:\n",
            "        if not self.population:\n",
            "            return 0\n",
            "        return int(max(run.get(\"generation\", 0) for run in self.population)) + 1\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def register_new_jobs(self, jobs_to_register: List[Dict[str, Any]]):\n",
            "        for job in jobs_to_register:\n",
            "            self.population.append(job)\n",
            "        self._save_ledger()\n",
            "        print(f\"[Hunter] Registered {len(jobs_to_register)} new jobs in ledger.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def process_generation_results(self, provenance_dir: str, job_hashes: List[str]):\n",
            "        print(f\"[Hunter] Processing {len(job_hashes)} new results from {provenance_dir}...\")\n",
            "        updated_count = 0\n",
            "        pop_lookup = {run[settings.HASH_KEY]: run for run in self.population}\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        for job_hash in job_hashes:\n",
            "            prov_file = os.path.join(provenance_dir, f\"provenance_{job_hash}.json\")\n",
            "            if not os.path.exists(prov_file):\n",
            "                continue\n",
            "            \n",
            "            try:\n",
            "                with open(prov_file, 'r') as f:\n",
            "                    provenance = json.load(f)\n",
            "                \n",
            "                run_to_update = pop_lookup.get(job_hash)\n",
            "                if not run_to_update:\n",
            "                    continue\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                # Extract metrics using .get() for safety\n",
            "                spec = provenance.get(\"spectral_fidelity\", {})\n",
            "                coherence = provenance.get(\"aletheia_metrics\", {})\n",
            "                geom = provenance.get(\"geometric_stability\", {})\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                sse = float(spec.get(settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY, 1002.0))\n",
            "                sse_null_a = float(spec.get(\"sse_null_phase_scramble\", 1002.0))\n",
            "                sse_null_b = float(spec.get(\"sse_null_target_shuffle\", 1002.0))\n",
            "                \n",
            "                # Calculate fitness\n",
            "                fitness = 0.0\n",
            "                if math.isfinite(sse) and sse < 900.0:\n",
            "                    base_fitness = 1.0 / (sse + 1e-9)\n",
            "                    delta_a = max(0, sse_null_a - sse)\n",
            "                    delta_b = max(0, sse_null_b - sse)\n",
            "                    falsifiability_bonus = LAMBDA_FALSIFIABILITY * (delta_a + delta_b)\n",
            "                    fitness = base_fitness + falsifiability_bonus\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "                # Update run fields\n",
            "                run_to_update[settings.SSE_METRIC_KEY] = sse\n",
            "                run_to_update[\"fitness\"] = fitness\n",
            "                run_to_update[\"pcs_score\"] = coherence.get(\"pcs_score\")\n",
            "                run_to_update[\"pli_score\"] = coherence.get(\"pli_score\")\n",
            "                run_to_update[\"ic_score\"] = coherence.get(\"ic_score\")\n",
            "                run_to_update[\"hamiltonian_norm_l2\"] = geom.get(settings.STABILITY_METRIC_KEY)\n",
            "                run_to_update[\"sse_null_phase_scramble\"] = sse_null_a\n",
            "                run_to_update[\"sse_null_target_shuffle\"] = sse_null_b\n",
            "                updated_count += 1\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "            except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, TypeError) as e:\n",
            "                print(f\"[Hunter Warning] Could not process {job_hash}: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
            "        \n",
            "        self._save_ledger()\n",
            "        print(f\"[Hunter] Successfully processed and updated {updated_count} runs.\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def get_next_generation(self, population_size: int) -> list:\n",
            "        print(f\"[Hunter] Breeding Generation {self.get_current_generation()}...\")\n",
            "        new_generation_params = []\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        # Filter for viable parents from previous generations\n",
            "        parents = [p for p in self.population if p.get(\"fitness\", 0) > 0]\n",
            "        if not parents:\n",
            "            print(\"[Hunter] No viable parents found. Generating random population.\")\n",
            "            for _ in range(population_size):\n",
            "                new_generation_params.append(self._create_random_params())\n",
            "            return new_generation_params\n",
            "        \n",
            "        parents.sort(key=lambda x: x[\"fitness\"], reverse=True)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        for _ in range(population_size):\n",
            "            # Tournament Selection\n",
            "            tournament = random.sample(parents, k=min(len(parents), TOURNAMENT_SIZE))\n",
            "            p1 = max(tournament, key=lambda x: x.get(\"fitness\", 0))\n",
            "            \n",
            "            tournament = random.sample(parents, k=min(len(parents), TOURNAMENT_SIZE))\n",
            "            p2 = max(tournament, key=lambda x: x.get(\"fitness\", 0))\n",
            "            \n",
            "            # Crossover and Mutation\n",
            "            child = self._crossover(p1, p2)\n",
            "            child = self._mutate(child)\n",
            "            new_generation_params.append(child)\n",
            "            \n",
            "        return new_generation_params\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def _create_random_params(self) -> dict:\n",
            "        return {key: random.uniform(val['min'], val['max']) for key, val in PARAM_SPACE.items()}\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def _crossover(self, p1: dict, p2: dict) -> dict:\n",
            "        child = {}\n",
            "        for key in PARAM_SPACE:\n",
            "            child[key] = random.choice([p1.get(key), p2.get(key)])\n",
            "        return child\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def _mutate(self, params: dict) -> dict:\n",
            "        mutated_params = params.copy()\n",
            "        for key, val_range in PARAM_SPACE.items():\n",
            "            if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
            "                perturbation = random.uniform(-MUTATION_STRENGTH, MUTATION_STRENGTH) * (val_range['max'] - val_range['min'])\n",
            "                mutated_params[key] = max(val_range['min'], min(val_range['max'], params.get(key, val_range['min']) + perturbation))\n",
            "        return mutated_params\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    def get_best_run(self) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
            "        valid_runs = [p for p in self.population if p.get(\"fitness\", 0) > 0]\n",
            "        if not valid_runs:\n",
            "            return None\n",
            "        return max(valid_runs, key=lambda x: x[\"fitness\"])\n",
            "The Hunter provides the high-level steering, but it is the HPC Core that performs the intensive physical calculations at the heart of the project.\n",
            "3. Phase 3: The High-Performance Computing (HPC) Core\n",
            "The HPC Core represents the computational heart of the IRER suite. In the V11.0 architecture, this layer is strictly reserved for the JAX-optimized physics loop. This design reflects the project's strategic scientific pivot to the Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) model, a critical move that decommissions the falsified BSSN solver. The SDG architecture enables a fully JAX-native, end-to-end differentiable simulation, allowing for the co-evolution of the informational field and the emergent geometry it sources—a foundational goal of the IRER framework.\n",
            "3.1. The Geometric Solver (solver_sdg.py)\n",
            "The solver_sdg.py module is the JAX-native geometric engine that replaces the previous BSSN solver. Its mandate is to calculate the emergent spacetime metric, g_mu_nu, sourced by the energy and momentum of the informational field. By implementing the SDG model's conformal ansatz, this component provides a computationally tractable yet physically grounded method for modeling how spacetime geometry is shaped by informational dynamics.\n",
            "#\n",
            "# solver_sdg.py\n",
            "# V11.0: SDG Geometric Solver (Phase 2 Core Upgrade)\n",
            "# Mandate: JAX-native implementation to replace falsified BSSN solver.\n",
            "# Physics: Solves scalar-tensor gravity sourced by T_info.\n",
            "#\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import jax\n",
            "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "@jax.jit\n",
            "def calculate_informational_stress_energy(\n",
            "    Psi: jnp.ndarray, params: dict\n",
            ") -> jnp.ndarray:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Calculates the Informational Stress-Energy Tensor (T_info).\n",
            "    MANDATE: T_info is the conserved tensor derived from L_FMIA.\n",
            "    It serves as the source term for the SDG geometric solver.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    sncgl_params = params.get(\"sncgl_params\", {})\n",
            "    lambda_nl = sncgl_params.get(\"sncgl_lambda\", 1.0)\n",
            "    rho = jnp.abs(Psi)**2\n",
            "    \n",
            "    # T_00 (Energy Density) is derived from L_FMIA potential and density\n",
            "    T_00 = (lambda_nl * rho**2) + 0.5 * rho\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # T_ij (Spatial Momentum/Stress) is simplified for this build\n",
            "    spatial_shape = Psi.shape\n",
            "    T_info = jnp.zeros((4, 4) + spatial_shape, dtype=Psi.dtype.real)\n",
            "    T_info = T_info.at[0, 0, ...].set(T_00)\n",
            "    \n",
            "    return T_info\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "@jax.jit\n",
            "def solve_sdg_geometry(\n",
            "    T_info: jnp.ndarray, current_rho_s: jnp.ndarray, params: dict\n",
            ") -> tuple[jnp.ndarray, jnp.ndarray]:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Solves the Spacetime-Density Gravity (SDG) field equations.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    sdg_params = params.get(\"sdg_params\", {})\n",
            "    sdg_alpha = sdg_params.get(\"sdg_alpha\", 0.5)\n",
            "    sdg_rho_vac = sdg_params.get(\"sdg_rho_vac\", 1.0)\n",
            "    \n",
            "    # --- Solve for the Spacetime Density Scalar (rho_s) ---\n",
            "    # Placeholder for solving DHOST-compliant field equations.\n",
            "    # Using a simple relaxation step: rho_s evolves based on energy sourcing.\n",
            "    T_00 = T_info[0, 0, ...]\n",
            "    dt_geom = 0.01 \n",
            "    k_coupling = 0.1\n",
            "    dissipation = 0.005\n",
            "    rho_s_update = dt_geom * (k_coupling * T_00 - dissipation * (current_rho_s - sdg_rho_vac))\n",
            "    new_rho_s = current_rho_s + rho_s_update\n",
            "    new_rho_s = jnp.clip(new_rho_s, 0.01, None)\n",
            "    \n",
            "    # --- Apply the Emergent Metric Ansatz ---\n",
            "    # g_mu_nu = (rho_vac / rho_s)^alpha * eta_mu_nu\n",
            "    eta_mu_nu_flat = jnp.diag(jnp.array([-1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0]))\n",
            "    scale_factor_A = (sdg_rho_vac / new_rho_s)**sdg_alpha\n",
            "    \n",
            "    # Broadcast scale factor A to the spatial dimensions of the metric\n",
            "    g_mu_nu = jnp.einsum('ij,...->ij...', eta_mu_nu_flat, jnp.ones_like(new_rho_s))\n",
            "    g_mu_nu *= scale_factor_A\n",
            "    \n",
            "    return new_rho_s, g_mu_nu\n",
            "3.2. The Physics Worker (worker_sncgl_sdg.py)\n",
            "The worker_sncgl_sdg.py module is the primary JAX physics engine. Its sole mandate is to execute a single, complete simulation run based on parameters provided by the orchestrator. The S-NCGL master equation contains several key physical coefficients, such as the non-local kernel width (sigma_k) and the linear growth rate (sncgl_epsilon), which are precisely the parameters that the aste_hunter module adjusts in its search for scientifically valid solutions. Crucially, the worker couples this evolution at every time step to the SDG solver, achieving a true field-geometry co-evolution. Upon completion, it generates the final data artifacts—the raw output of the simulation—which are passed downstream for validation.\n",
            "#\n",
            "# worker_sncgl_sdg.py\n",
            "# V11.0: S-NCGL Physics Worker with SDG Coupling\n",
            "# Mandate: Implement S-NCGL EOM coupled with SDG solver, using received UUID.\n",
            "#\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import jax\n",
            "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
            "import numpy as np\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import argparse\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import h5py\n",
            "import time\n",
            "from typing import Any\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "from solver_sdg import solve_sdg_geometry, calculate_informational_stress_energy\n",
            "import settings\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def apply_complex_diffusion(\n",
            "    Psi: jnp.ndarray, g_mu_nu: jnp.ndarray, k_sq: jnp.ndarray\n",
            ") -> jnp.ndarray:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Placeholder for metric-aware complex diffusion (covariant Laplacian).\n",
            "    For this build, we use a simplified form where the metric scales the flat laplacian.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    D_real = 0.5\n",
            "    c1_imag = 0.8\n",
            "    Omega_sq = g_mu_nu[1, 1, ...] # Extract from g_xx\n",
            "    g_inv_sq = 1.0 / (Omega_sq + 1e-9)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # Spectral laplacian in flat space, scaled by inverse metric\n",
            "    psi_k = jnp.fft.fft2(Psi)\n",
            "    laplacian_psi_k = -k_sq * psi_k\n",
            "    flat_laplacian_psi = jnp.fft.ifft2(laplacian_psi_k).real\n",
            "    \n",
            "    return (D_real + 1j * c1_imag) * flat_laplacian_psi * g_inv_sq\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def apply_non_local_term(\n",
            "    Psi: jnp.ndarray, gaussian_kernel_k: jnp.ndarray\n",
            ") -> jnp.ndarray:\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    Implements the Non-Local 'Splash' Term via spectral convolution.\n",
            "    \"\"\"\n",
            "    nu = 1.0 # Non-local coupling constant\n",
            "    rho = jnp.abs(Psi)**2\n",
            "    rho_k = jnp.fft.fft2(rho)\n",
            "    convolved_k = rho_k * gaussian_kernel_k\n",
            "    non_local_potential = jnp.fft.ifft2(convolved_k).real\n",
            "    \n",
            "    return -nu * non_local_potential * Psi\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "@jax.jit\n",
            "def _evolve_sncgl_step(\n",
            "    carry: tuple, _: Any\n",
            ") -> tuple:\n",
            "    \"\"\" One step of the coupled S-NCGL/SDG co-evolution. \"\"\"\n",
            "    Psi, rho_s, g_mu_nu, params, k_sq, gaussian_kernel_k = carry\n",
            "    \n",
            "    sncgl_params = params.get(\"sncgl_params\", {})\n",
            "    sim_params = params.get(\"simulation\", {})\n",
            "    \n",
            "    epsilon = sncgl_params.get(\"sncgl_epsilon\", 0.1)\n",
            "    lambda_nl = sncgl_params.get(\"sncgl_lambda\", 1.0)\n",
            "    dt = sim_params.get(\"dt\", 0.01)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- 1. S-NCGL EOM Terms ---\n",
            "    L_term = epsilon * Psi\n",
            "    NL_term = (1.0 + 1j * 0.0) * jnp.abs(Psi)**2 * Psi * lambda_nl\n",
            "    Diff_term = apply_complex_diffusion(Psi, g_mu_nu, k_sq)\n",
            "    NonL_term = apply_non_local_term(Psi, gaussian_kernel_k)\n",
            "    \n",
            "    dPsi_dt = L_term + Diff_term - NL_term - NonL_term\n",
            "    Psi_new = Psi + dt * dPsi_dt\n",
            "    \n",
            "    # --- 2. Geometric Feedback Loop ---\n",
            "    T_info = calculate_informational_stress_energy(Psi_new, params)\n",
            "    rho_s_new, g_mu_nu_new = solve_sdg_geometry(T_info, rho_s, params)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    new_carry = (Psi_new, rho_s_new, g_mu_nu_new, params, k_sq, gaussian_kernel_k)\n",
            "    history_slice = Psi_new.real # Store real part of psi for history\n",
            "    return new_carry, history_slice\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def run_sncgl_sdg_coevolution(run_uuid: str, config_path: str):\n",
            "    with open(config_path, 'r') as f:\n",
            "        params = json.load(f)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    print(f\"Starting co-evolution for UUID: {run_uuid}\")\n",
            "    \n",
            "    sim_cfg = params.get(\"simulation\", {})\n",
            "    sncgl_cfg = params.get(\"sncgl_params\", {})\n",
            "    N = sim_cfg.get(\"N_grid\", 32)\n",
            "    T_steps = sim_cfg.get(\"T_steps\", 200)\n",
            "    key = jax.random.PRNGKey(params.get(\"global_seed\", 42))\n",
            "    \n",
            "    # --- Initialize fields ---\n",
            "    Psi_initial = jax.random.uniform(key, (N, N), dtype=jnp.complex64) * 0.1\n",
            "    rho_s_initial = jnp.ones((N, N)) * params.get(\"sdg_params\", {}).get(\"sdg_rho_vac\", 1.0)\n",
            "    eta_mu_nu = jnp.diag(jnp.array([-1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0]))\n",
            "    g_mu_nu_initial = jnp.tile(eta_mu_nu[:, :, None, None], (1, 1, N, N))\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- Pre-compute spectral grids ---\n",
            "    k_freq = jnp.fft.fftfreq(N, d=1.0/N) * 2.0 * jnp.pi\n",
            "    kx, ky = jnp.meshgrid(k_freq, k_freq, indexing='ij')\n",
            "    k_sq = kx**2 + ky**2\n",
            "    sigma_k = sncgl_cfg.get(\"sigma_k\", 2.5)\n",
            "    gaussian_kernel_k = jnp.exp(-k_sq / (2 * sigma_k**2))\n",
            "    \n",
            "    # --- Run simulation using lax.scan ---\n",
            "    initial_carry = (Psi_initial, rho_s_initial, g_mu_nu_initial, params, k_sq, gaussian_kernel_k)\n",
            "    final_carry, psi_history = jax.lax.scan(_evolve_sncgl_step, initial_carry, None, length=T_steps)\n",
            "    final_psi, final_rho_s, final_g, _, _, _ = final_carry\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "    # --- Save Artifacts ---\n",
            "    output_filename = f\"simulation_data_{run_uuid}.h5\"\n",
            "    output_path = os.path.join(settings.DATA_DIR, output_filename)\n",
            "    os.makedirs(settings.DATA_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n",
            "    with h5py.File(output_path, 'w') as f:\n",
            "        f.create_dataset('psi_history', data=np.array(psi_history))\n",
            "        f.create_dataset('final_psi_field', data=np.array(final_psi))\n",
            "        f.create_dataset('final_metric_g_munu', data=np.array(final_g))\n",
            "        f.attrs['job_uuid'] = run_uuid\n",
            "        f.attrs['global_seed'] = params.get(\"global_seed\")\n",
            "    print(f\"Worker artifact saved to {output_path}\")\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n",
            "    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"S-NCGL+SDG Worker\")\n",
            "    parser.add_argument(\"--params\", required=True, help=\"Path to config JSON file\")\n",
            "    parser.add_argument(\"--job_uuid\", required=True, help=\"The unified job_uuid\")\n",
            "    args = parser.parse_args()\n",
            "    \n",
            "    start_time = time.time()\n",
            "    run_sncgl_sdg_coevolution(args.job_uuid, args.params)\n",
            "    duration = time.time() - start_time\n",
            "    print(f\"Worker {args.job_uuid[:8]} complete in {duration:.2f}s.\")\n",
            "The data artifacts produced by the HPC core are raw numerical outputs; they must now be rigorously analyzed and validated to generate auditable provenance.\n",
            "4. Phase 4: Validation & Provenance (validation_pipeline.py)\n",
            "The validation_pipeline.py module serves a critical scientific governance function as the \"Auditor\" of the IRER suite. Its role is to ingest the raw simulation artifacts generated by the Worker and perform a rigorous, multi-faceted analysis. This analysis transforms raw data into scientific fact by calculating metrics for spectral fidelity, structural coherence, and geometric stability. The final output is a cryptographically-bound provenance.json artifact—the immutable \"birth certificate\" for each simulation run—that provides a complete and auditable record of the experiment's intent and outcome.\n",
            "#\n",
            "# validation_pipeline.py\n",
            "# CLASSIFICATION: Spectral Fidelity & Provenance Module (V11.0)\n",
            "# GOAL: Serves as the immutable source of truth that binds experimental\n",
            "#       intent (parameters) to scientific fact (metrics).\n",
            "#\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import json\n",
            "import hashlib\n",
            "import sys\n",
            "import os\n",
            "import argparse\n",
            "import h5py\n",
            "import numpy as np\n",
            "from scipy.signal import coherence, find_peaks\n",
            "from scipy.stats import entropy\n",
            "import random\n",
            "from typing import Optional\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "import settings\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def generate_canonical_hash(params: dict) -> str:\n",
            "    \"\"\"Creates a deterministic SHA-256 hash from the core physics parameters.\"\"\"\n",
            "    sim_params = params.get(\"sncgl_params\", {})\n",
            "    canonical_string = json.dumps(sim_params, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':'))\n",
            "    return hashlib.sha256(canonical_string.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def calculate_log_prime_sse(psi_field_data: np.ndarray, custom_targets: Optional[np.ndarray] = None) -> dict:\n",
            "    \"\"\"Calculates Spectral Stress Error by matching peaks to logarithmic primes.\"\"\"\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        fft_result = np.fft.fft2(psi_field_data)\n",
            "        magnitude_spectrum = np.abs(np.fft.fftshift(fft_result)).flatten()\n",
            "        \n",
            "        # Ensure freqs array has the same length as the flattened spectrum\n",
            "        N = len(magnitude_spectrum)\n",
            "        freqs = np.fft.fftshift(np.fft.fftfreq(N))\n",
            "        \n",
            "        peaks_idx, _ = find_peaks(magnitude_spectrum, height=np.max(magnitude_spectrum) * 0.1, distance=5)\n",
            "        if len(peaks_idx) == 0:\n",
            "            return {\"log_prime_sse\": 999.0, \"failure_reason\": \"No peaks found\"}\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        observed_k = np.sort(np.abs(freqs[peaks_idx]))\n",
            "        \n",
            "        if custom_targets is not None:\n",
            "            targets_k = custom_targets\n",
            "        else:\n",
            "            primes = np.array([2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19])\n",
            "            targets_k = np.log(primes)\n",
            "        \n",
            "        if observed_k.size > 0 and targets_k.size > 0:\n",
            "            scaling_factor = targets_k[0] / observed_k[0]\n",
            "            observed_k *= scaling_factor\n",
            "        \n",
            "        sse = 0\n",
            "        if observed_k.size > 0:\n",
            "            for target in targets_k:\n",
            "                sse += np.min((observed_k - target)**2)\n",
            "            sse /= len(targets_k)\n",
            "        else:\n",
            "            sse = 1001.0 # Error if no observed peaks to match\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "        return {\"log_prime_sse\": sse, \"failure_reason\": None}\n",
            "    except Exception:\n",
            "        return {\"log_prime_sse\": 1002.0, \"failure_reason\": \"Computational error\"}\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def _null_a_phase_scramble(rho: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:\n",
            "    \"\"\"Null A: Scrambles the phases in Fourier space, preserving the amplitude spectrum.\"\"\"\n",
            "    F = np.fft.fftn(rho)\n",
            "    amps = np.abs(F)\n",
            "    phases = np.random.uniform(0, 2 * np.pi, F.shape)\n",
            "    F_scr = amps * np.exp(1j * phases)\n",
            "    return np.fft.ifftn(F_scr).real\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def _null_b_target_shuffle(targets: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:\n",
            "    \"\"\"Null B: Shuffles the log-prime targets to test for spurious matches.\"\"\"\n",
            "    shuffled_targets = list(targets)\n",
            "    random.shuffle(shuffled_targets)\n",
            "    return np.asarray(shuffled_targets)\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def calculate_pcs(rho_history: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
            "    \"\"\"Calculates the Phase Coherence Score.\"\"\"\n",
            "    try:\n",
            "        h, w = rho_history.shape[1], rho_history.shape[2]\n",
            "        ray_1 = rho_history[:, h//4, w//4]\n",
            "        ray_2 = rho_history[:, 3*h//4, 3*w//4]\n",
            "        _, Cxy = coherence(ray_1, ray_2, nperseg=len(ray_1)//2)\n",
            "        pcs_score = np.mean(Cxy)\n",
            "        return float(pcs_score) if not np.isnan(pcs_score) else 0.0\n",
            "    except Exception:\n",
            "        return 0.0\n",
            "\n",
            "\n",
            "def calculate_pli(rho_final_state: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
            "    \"\"\"Calculates the Principled Localization Index (IPR).\"\"\"\n",
            "    try:\n",
